principle: Never stop at the feature or even the benefit — trace the means-end chain to the life outcome the customer actually craves
explanation: X.copy|means_end_chain: feature->functional_benefit->emotional_benefit->life_value; chain_example: "256-bit_AES"->"data_is_secure"->"no_fear_of_breach"->"you_protect_the_company_you_built"; most_copy_stops_at_functional_benefit; conversion_lives_at_life_value_level; method: ask_"so_what?"_3x_per_feature; NO:stopping_at_spec NO:stopping_at_functional; eg: "auto-backup" >> "auto-backup(feat)->never_lose_work(func)->no_panic_at_3am(emo)->you_are_a_professional_who_ships(value)"

principle: Replace sign-up form headers with the benefit users gain and address their top fears inline before they need to ask
explanation: X.copy|signup_form_microcopy: "Create_account"/"Sign_up"/"Register" -> describe_action NOT motivation; reframe_title: what_user_gets ("Join_12000_builders" OR "Start_building_free"); address_fears_inline: spam ("No spam") + privacy ("We_never_share_your_data") + commitment ("No_credit_card_required"); NO:"Create_account"_as_headline NO:form_with_no_reassurance; eg: "Create_Account" >> "Start_building_for_free" + inline_badge: "No_credit_card · Cancel_anytime · No_spam"

principle: Keep the above-the-fold section free of navigation links to other pages — every link that leads off the landing page is a conversion leak
explanation: X.copy|lp_navigation_leak: landing_page_goal: one_action_only; navigation_menu: offers_escape_routes -> visitor_leaves_to_explore -> conversion_drops; research: removing_nav_from_paid_landing_pages_consistently_increases_conversion(Unbounce+VWO_case_studies); NO:full_site_nav_on_paid_ad_landing_page; eg: full_nav_header >> logo_only+minimal_footer

principle: Tell a story instead of giving advice — stories create mental simulations that let readers rehearse the solution before they need it, making them remember and act
explanation: X.copy|story_as_simulation: Heath: story = flight_simulator_for_the_brain; reader_mentally_simulates_events -> emotional_response = almost_real; advice_tells_WHAT_to_do; NO:bullet_list_of_benefits_where_a_story_fits; eg: "Our_software_has_great_support" >> "Maria_was_3h_from_a_client_demo_when_her_import_failed — here's_what_happened"

principle: Add a click trigger beside the primary CTA — one short sentence near the button that resolves a final objection or adds an immediate reason to act
explanation: X.copy|click_trigger: click_trigger==short_text_NEAR_button NOT on_it; function: resolve_last_concern OR add_urgency OR reinforce_value; distinct_from_button_text: button=value; NO:no_copy_next_to_high_stakes_CTA; eg: [Start_free_trial] alone >> [Start_free_trial] + "No_credit_card — cancel_anytime"_below

principle: Map every product benefit to one of the eight Life-Force desires to activate hardwired motivation — rational features alone never move people to buy
explanation: X.copy|life_force_8: LF8_biogenic: (1)survival+enjoyment_of_life (2)food+drink (3)freedom_from_fear+pain+danger (4)sexual_companionship (5)comfortable_living (6)social_superiority (7)care_for_loved_ones (8)social_approval; these_are_PRE-WIRED_not_learned; mapping: product_benefit->nearest_LF8_desire->lead_with_that_emotion; NO:feature_list_without_desire_anchor NO:only_rational_benefits; eg: "encrypted_backup" >> "sleep_knowing_your_family_photos_are_safe_forever"(LF3+LF7)

principle: Back evaluations with contractable facts — but empty facts unknown to reader are useless
explanation: X.copy|facts_for_eval: fact == something_you_can_put_in_contract; reader_draws_own_conclusion_from_facts -> stronger_than_your_evaluation; BUT unknown_fact == empty; NO:citing_obscure_ratings/acronyms eg: "quality_content" >> "articles_by_Business_School_professors"; "member_of_RSVA_ICC" -> means_nothing_to_reader

principle: Replace bureaucratic text with human specifics — day, time, duration, fallback plan
explanation: X.copy|bureaucratic_to_human: formal_template -> human_text_with_specifics; add: weekday + full_date + duration + what_to_do_if_problem; human_text -support_calls; NO:vague_deadlines NO:abbreviations_without_explanation eg: "cold/hot_water_off_until_done" >> "water_off_MON_11may_11am_4-6hrs_call_XXX_if_no_water_after_5pm"

principle: Write a tagline not a motto — six to eight words of specific differentiation and concrete benefit that only this site can claim
explanation: X.copy|tagline_formula: tagline!=motto; motto=generic_aspiration(any_company_could_say); tagline=specific_unique_value_only_this_site_claims; NO:"We_bring_good_things_to_life"(motto+vague); eg: "Work.Wisely"(too_short+uninformative) >> "Help_for_the_business_of_running_your_small_business"

principle: Stories work because we calibrate our lives by watching others — fake characters fail, products can't be heroes
explanation: X.copy|story_calibration: humans_use_stories_to_calibrate: "how_do_others_live?_am_I_doing_it_right?"; hero == real_person_with_real_actions; fake_characters -> no_calibration -> reader_disengages; product_as_hero == boring; case_study: show_people_overcoming_obstacles_not_product_features; NO:"Masha_on_regular_chair_Sasha_on_ergonomic" eg: "CRM_improved_service" >> "managers_rebelled_against_new_system_—_CEO_handled_it_by..."

principle: Two types of useful action: solve a problem or provoke an emotion — informing is NOT a useful action
explanation: X.copy|action_types: pragmatic: reader_has_problem -> searches_solution -> already_motivated; emotional: fear/outrage/curiosity/pride/nostalgia/joy; if_no_problem_AND_no_emotion -> why_read?; NO:"inform" == not_useful_action; "50_new_features" -> nobody_opens eg: "program_update_announcement" >> "5_ways_to_simplify_salon_admin_work"

principle: Paragraph structure: one theme, first sentence declares it for scanning, last sentence bridges to next
explanation: X.copy|paragraph_structure: one_paragraph == one_theme; first_sentence == declaration_of_topic + keywords_early; last_sentence == conclusion + bridge_to_next; 3-9_lines_optimal; NO:starting_with_"for_example"/"however"/"in_this_case" eg: "And_vice_versa:_if_you..." >> "To_choose_headphones_answer_these_questions:..."

principle: Structure a case study as situation, complication, resolution, and quantified result — a project description without a before-and-after narrative is a portfolio piece, not a sales tool
explanation: X.copy|case_study_structure: project_description: what_we_built; no_tension_no_resolution; SCR_structure: (1)situation: who_the_client_is+their_context (2)complication: what_was_broken_and_why_it_mattered (3)resolution: what_we_specifically_did+why_that_approach (4)result: quantified_outcome+timeline; NO:listing_deliverables_without_outcome; eg: "We_redesigned_their_website" >> problem+approach+"conversion_up_40%_in_6_weeks"

principle: Conclusion: flip the modality — objective article ends with personal take, opinion piece ends with cold statistics
explanation: X.copy|flip_conclusion: objective_article -> end_with_personal_opinion; opinion_piece -> end_with_statistics; scary_article -> end_with_"but_if_you_dont..."; positive_article -> end_with_honest_caveat; NO:repeating_subheadings_as_summary (reader_feels_insulted); "look_from_other_side" -> bodes_well eg: "Canada_downsides:_medicine,_career,_banks" conclusion >> "despite_all_this_Canada_is_great_for_those_who..."

principle: Offer exactly three pricing tiers and visually elevate the middle one — three options satisfy choice without paralysis, and the elevated middle tier captures the majority of conversions
explanation: X.copy|pricing_three_tiers: psychology: three_options_optimal(two=no_context; four+=paralysis); middle_tier_effect: upper_option_makes_middle_seem_reasonable; lower_option_makes_middle_seem_quality; visual_elevation: border+badge("Most_popular")+color_highlight+slight_scale; price_ratio: middle_should_be_~2-3x_basic; NO:two_tiers_only(no_anchor) NO:four+_tiers NO:all_tiers_same_visual_weight; eg: Basic$29+Pro$79+Enterprise_quote >> Basic$29+Pro$79(highlighted)+Enterprise$199

principle: Show readers their own face in the copy — recognition of self precedes desire, and readers buy from copy that mirrors their exact situation back at them
explanation: X.copy|identification_technique: Schwartz_stage5: reader_sees_themselves_in_copy -> feels_"this_was_written_for_me" -> trust+desire_spike; method: name_specific_identity_marker(role+frustration+failed_attempt+aspiration) -> mirror_it_in_headline_or_lead; vague_ICP -> nobody_identifies; NO:generic_demographic("business_owners"); eg: "For_entrepreneurs" >> "For_solo_founders_on_their_second_SaaS_tired_of_context-switching"

principle: Same word can be jargon or precise term — keep when it's the real discipline, cut when it's decoration
explanation: X.copy|term_vs_jargon: word_in_its_discipline == precise_term -> keep; same_word_outside_discipline == decoration -> replace; NO:blanket_banning_complex_words; context_decides eg: "audit_of_consumer_patterns" (jargon) vs "audit_by_accounting_firm" (term); "dominant_theme" (jargon) vs "dominant_gene" (biology)

principle: A mission statement competitors could copy is a cliché — genuine culture produces unique wording
explanation: X.copy|mission_cliche: "best_service/reliability/confidence" -> any_company_could_say_this; real_culture -> unique_phrasing_competitor_cant_steal; test: would_this_fit_a_rival? YES -> cliche; NO:generic_aspirational_statements eg: "provide_best_service_to_clients" >> "no_tenders_no_conveyor_handmade_work_for_companies_who_love_what_they_do"

principle: End every article with a contextual CTA tied to the article topic, not a generic newsletter signup — a reader who just finished an SEO article converts better on an SEO audit offer than on a generic email list
explanation: X.copy|article_end_cta: generic_newsletter_at_end: disconnected_from_what_reader_just_learned -> low_conversion; contextual_CTA: match_the_offer_to_the_article_topic; implementation: relevant_lead_magnet+inline_before_last_paragraph+repeat_after; position: above_comments+related_articles; copy: references_problem_covered_in_article; NO:same_CTA_on_every_article NO:CTA_only_in_sidebar; eg: SEO_article_ends_with_"Subscribe_to_newsletter" >> SEO_article_ends_with_"Get_our_free_SEO_audit_checklist"

principle: Demonstrate sensory qualities instead of describing them — text handles specs, visuals handle emotions
explanation: X.copy|demonstration: feelings_cant_be_written_convincingly; "sharp_knife" -> show_chef_cutting; "creative_freedom" -> show_person_creating; text_role: specs + facts; visual_role: emotions + identity; NO:writing_feelings_as_adjectives eg: "premium_backpacks_for_adventurous_spirits" >> "backpacks_for_forest_steppe_desert" + photos_of_confident_people

principle: Stack micro-commitments before the main ask — a small public yes creates identity pressure to stay consistent with larger future yeses
explanation: X.copy|commitment_ladder: Cialdini: small_public_commitment -> self-image_alignment -> consistency_pressure_toward_larger_action; ladder: small_yes(quiz/checkbox) -> medium_yes(trial/download) -> purchase; each_step_reinforces_identity("I'm_the_kind_of_person_who_does_X"); progress_indicators_amplify_consistency; NO:large_ask_first NO:no_visible_commitment_trail NO:contradicting_stated_values_at_checkout; eg: "Buy_now"(cold) >> quiz_"What's_your_#1_challenge?" -> results_page -> relevant_CTA

principle: Anchor every price against a higher reference point before revealing it — the contrast principle makes any number feel smaller or larger based solely on what preceded it
explanation: X.copy|contrast_anchoring: Cialdini: perception_is_relative_NOT_absolute; first_number_seen = anchor -> all_subsequent_numbers_measured_against_it; pricing: show_highest_tier_first OR original_price_before_discount; NO:leading_with_cheapest_option; eg: pricing_table_basic->pro->enterprise >> enterprise->pro->basic(pro_feels_like_savings)

principle: Text about yourself is not about you — it's about your utility; reader has 50 tabs open, not a romantic dinner with you
explanation: X.copy|about_you_paradox: reader_seeks_function_not_soul; vanity_facts: "180_classrooms_40_cities_90_programs" -> impresses_company NOT reader; replace_with_reader_scenario; match_readers_request_fast; recruiter_has_50_candidates; once_utility_proven -> then_personality; NO:creative_journey_openers NO:scale_facts_without_benefit eg: "10_years_180_classrooms_40_cities" >> "classrooms_near_your_office_so_you_come_after_work"

principle: Repeat the primary CTA at least three times on a long landing page — first above the fold for ready buyers, second after social proof, third at the bottom after all objections are resolved
explanation: X.copy|cta_repetition: buyer_types: (1)ready_now: buys_above_fold (2)skeptical: needs_proof_first (3)thorough: reads_everything_then_buys; single_CTA: serves_only_ready-now_buyers; three_CTAs: same_offer_different_page_context; CTA_wording: can_vary("Start_free_trial"above_fold; "Try_free_for_14_days"after_features; "Get_started_today"at_bottom); NO:CTA_only_above_fold_on_long_page NO:three_different_offers_in_three_CTAs; eg: one_CTA_top >> CTA_after_hero+after_social_proof+after_FAQ

principle: Concrete actions not abstract processes — citizens think in events, bureaucrats in programs
explanation: X.copy|actions_not_processes: "implementation_of_support_program" -> abstract; "city_gave_money_to_entrepreneurs" -> concrete; start_with_specific_event -> mention_program_later; NO:leading_with_systems_concepts_programs eg: "within_framework_of_program_various_measures_taken" >> "city_hosted_small_business_fair._Entrepreneurs_met_investors._Part_of_support_program"

principle: Combine the unexpected opener with the concrete payoff — surprise earns attention for one sentence, concrete sensory detail converts that attention into memory
explanation: X.copy|unexpected_plus_concrete: Heath_SUCCESs: U+C_combo: unexpected_alone -> attention_but_no_retention; concrete_alone -> memorable_but_unread; together: unexpected_gap_OPENS -> concrete_detail_FILLS_AND_STICKS; NO:unexpected_without_concrete_followup; eg: generic_tip_article >> "Why_the_worst_copywriters_write_more" + "Every_deleted_draft_is_a_3-minute_attention_span_sharpener"

principle: Specificity collapses possibility space — that's the point, it forces real implementation thinking
explanation: X.copy|collapse_space: abstract_"comfort" -> everyone_imagines_own_version; concrete_example -> collapses_to_specific_meaning; readers_ask_"is_that_all?"; this_is_GOOD: forces_real_planning; NO:staying_abstract_to_avoid_hard_questions eg: "raise_quality_of_life" >> "students_furnish_apartment_in_one_trip_for_cheap"

principle: Cutting noise makes text cleaner not better — better needs substance added
explanation: X.copy|clean_vs_useful: cut_noise -> cleaner (NOT better NOT more_interesting); better == add_substance; two_separate_steps; NO:stop_after_cutting eg: "loan_on_best_terms" -> cleaned_to_nothing -> filled: "cash_loan_at_X%_card_delivered_to_home_or_office"

principle: Place two CTAs on every page — one direct for buyers ready now, one transitional for buyers still deciding — missing the transitional loses the majority
explanation: X.copy|two_cta_types: direct_CTA: high_commitment(Schedule+Buy+Request_Quote); transitional_CTA: low_commitment(Download_checklist+Watch_demo+Free_consultation); most_visitors: NOT_ready_to_buy_today -> need_transitional; transitional_also: builds_email_list+nurtures; direct_CTA: prominent_above_fold; transitional: secondary_nearby; NO:only_direct_CTA NO:"Learn_More"_as_both NO:no_CTA_at_all; eg: "Contact_Us"_only >> "Schedule_Inspection"(direct)+"Download_Free_Guide"(transitional)

principle: Establish shared identity before any persuasion attempt — 'we are the same kind of people' bypasses skepticism before a single claim is made
explanation: X.copy|unity_preframe: Cialdini_7th: shared_identity -> "we"_frame -> all_subsequent_copy_feels_internal_not_external; identity_markers: shared_profession+shared_struggle+shared_enemy+shared_values; placement: headline_or_subhead_names_the_tribe_BEFORE_pitch; NO:pitching_before_establishing_shared_identity; eg: generic_product_page >> "Built_by_engineers_who_hated_BI_dashboards — for_engineers_who_feel_the_same"

principle: Keep brand voice constant but vary tone per context — voice is who you are, tone is how you feel right now
explanation: X.copy|voice_vs_tone: voice==fixed_personality_across_all_touchpoints; tone==situational_adaptation: success->celebratory; error->empathetic+calm; warning->serious; loading->reassuring; test: read_error_msg+success_msg -> same_personality_different_mood == correct; NO:celebratory_tone_on_error_page NO:legal_tone_on_success_screen NO:identical_flat_tone_everywhere; eg: "ERROR_0x4F2_Transaction_failed" >> "Something_went_wrong_on_our_end._Your_card_was_not_charged._Please_try_again."

principle: Person + does + this way — four-step algorithm to turn fog into cinema
explanation: X.copy|person_does: 1_find_the_action; 2_find_who_does_it; 3_formulate_action_in_clear_words; 4_check_for_conclusion; NO:abstract_noun_piles_without_actors eg: "comfort_of_visitors_is_our_priority" >> "admin_offers_coffee_enters_client_data_walks_to_car_with_umbrella"

principle: Complex synonyms add no meaning — replace with the simplest word that carries the same idea
explanation: X.copy|complex_synonyms: same_concept often has simple + complex forms; complex != smarter; pick_shortest_familiar_word; NO:using_latin_root_when_native_exists eg: "personnel_resource_demonstrated_positive_productivity_tendency" >> "team_started_closing_deals_faster"

principle: Use scarcity only when the limit is real and the consequence of missing out is explicitly named — fake scarcity permanently destroys trust once detected
explanation: X.copy|real_scarcity: Cialdini: perceived_deprivation -> loss_aversion -> action; loss_framing > gain_framing; real_scarcity_types: (1)quantity("3_spots_remain") (2)time("closes_Friday") (3)access("cohort_capped_at_25"); NO:"limited_time"_without_deadline; eg: "Limited_time_offer" >> "Cohort_closes_Friday — capped_at_25_to_preserve_1:1_feedback"

principle: Build each service on a services page as a mini-landing page: problem it solves, how you deliver it, who it is for, and a specific CTA — a bullet list of service names answers none of the visitor's questions
explanation: X.copy|services_page_structure: service_name_only: visitor_cannot_evaluate_fit; mini_LP_per_service: (1)problem_name: "Struggling_with_X?" (2)solution_mechanism: how_you_deliver (3)ideal_client: "perfect_for_companies_that..." (4)outcome: specific_result (5)CTA: specific_to_service; layout: accordion_or_separate_sections_per_service; NO:services_as_flat_icon_grid NO:no_CTA_per_service; eg: "Web_Design / SEO / Branding" grid >> each_service_with_problem+method+CTA

principle: Passive voice to active — but keep the real hero, don't just swap mechanically
explanation: X.copy|passive_to_active: "was_done_by_X" -> "X_did"; BUT check_who_is_hero; if_hero_shifts_after_swap -> restructure_differently; NO:mechanical_swap_losing_focus eg: "houses_were_built_per_standard" >> NOT "builder_built_houses" >> "houses_meet_standard_X"

principle: Show the cost of doing nothing as concrete facts not emotional drama — stakes are salt in a recipe, a small necessary amount, not the whole meal
explanation: X.copy|storybrand_stakes: stakes==what_customer_loses_by_not_acting; format: real_consequences_in_concrete_terms(small_leak->$15k_damage; no_CRM->projects_slip+team_loses_trust); three_layers: practical(money/time)+emotional(stress/guilt)+philosophical(who_you_are); NO:catastrophizing_drama NO:fake_urgency NO:skipping_stakes_entirely(no_urgency); eg: "Act_now"(vague) >> "A_small_leak_today_becomes_$15k_water_damage_in_a_year"

principle: Attach a 'because' clause to every call to action — Langer's compliance experiment shows adding any reason raises response rates even when the reason is obvious
explanation: X.copy|because_justification: Langer_1978: adding_"because"+reason -> compliance_rises_regardless_of_reason_quality; brain_heuristic: "because"_keyword -> automatic_compliance_trigger; CTA_pattern: every_ask_gets_a_because(benefit+OR+process+OR+scarcity); NO:bare_CTA_no_justification NO:"click_here"_only NO:CTA_with_vague_reason("because_we're_great"); eg: "Schedule_a_call" >> "Schedule_a_call — because_30_minutes_shows_you_exactly_what_to_fix_first"

principle: Write error messages that name the specific problem and tell the user exactly what to do next — never use the words 'error', 'failed', or 'invalid' alone
explanation: X.copy|error_message_formula: clinical_labels("Error"+"Failed"+"Invalid") -> user_feels_blamed+confused; formula: specific_cause + concrete_next_step; tone: conversational_NOT_legal; user_is_never_wrong -> mismatch_or_system_is; NO:"Invalid_input" NO:"Error_403" NO:"Operation_failed"; eg: "Invalid_password" >> "Password must be 8+ chars — try adding a number or symbol"; "Connection_error" >> "Can't connect right now. Check your internet and try again"

principle: Numbers in text are pain — put data into tables, processes into diagrams, products into photos; readers spend most time on visuals
explanation: X.copy|visualize_data: numbers_in_prose -> reader_drowns_counting_digits; table: same_data + less_space + easy_comparison + color_for_highlights; diagrams: processes + dependencies; photos/screenshots: physical_products + interfaces; readers_spend_most_time_on_visuals_in_documents; NO:describing_in_words_what_can_be_shown eg: "Q1_shipments_20.1M_98%_plan_Q2_19M_96%_Q3_24.4M_105%" >> table_with_3_rows_and_columns: quarter|shipments|vs_plan|vs_year

principle: Three text qualities: brief means no meaningless words, clear means understood first read, convincing means facts and numbers
explanation: X.copy|three_qualities: brief (cut_words_without_meaning) + clear (understood_on_first_read) + convincing (facts + numbers); three_separate_skills; NO:verbose_formality NO:abstract_claims eg: "in_advance_no_later_than" >> "in_2_days"; "unforeseen_circumstances" >> "contractor_miscalculated_budget"

principle: Name subscription forms by the content they deliver, not by the act of subscribing — specificity converts better than generic promises
explanation: X.copy|newsletter_copy_formula: title_"Subscribe_to_newsletter" -> describes_action NOT value_received; rename: what_arrives+frequency+benefit; CTA_mirrors_title: "Get_weekly_tips" NOT "Subscribe"; add_social_proof: "Join_12000_designers"; frequency: "Every_Tuesday" beats "Regularly"; NO:title="Subscribe" NO:CTA="Sign_up_for_updates"; eg: "Subscribe_to_newsletter" >> "Get_a_UI_trick_every_week — join_8400_designers"; CTA:"Subscribe" >> "Send_me_the_tips"

principle: Address all three problem layers — external is what broke, internal is how it feels, philosophical is why it matters — brands that name only the external miss the sale
explanation: X.copy|three_problem_layers: external: practical_observable(AC_broken+slow_software); internal: emotional_pain_it_causes(panic+guilt+feeling_out_of_control); philosophical: why_this_matters_in_life(home_should_be_safe+teams_deserve_trust); customers_BUY_to_solve_external BUT_motivated_by_internal+philosophical; NO:external_problem_only NO:ignoring_emotional_layer; eg: "AC_broken"_only >> "AC_broken(ext)+family_panicking(int)+home_should_be_refuge(phil)"

principle: Write button text from the user's perspective — label it with what they receive, not with what the system does
explanation: X.copy|button_value_formula: button_text==system_action -> impersonal; button_text==user_outcome -> converts_better; Aagaard: Value+Relevance=Conversions; formula: [user_verb]+[what_they_receive]; NO:"Submit" NO:"Download" NO:"Register" NO:"Send"; eg: "Submit" >> "Get_my_free_guide"; "Register" >> "Start_my_free_trial"; "Download" >> "Download_the_checklist"; "Send" >> "Send_my_question"

principle: If the text fits any audience it's a cliché — specificity creates memorability
explanation: X.copy|cliche_test: text_works_for_anyone == touches_no_one; write_for_specific_people/context -> remembered; generic_graduation_speech -> forgotten; speech_about_THIS_class -> remembered_for_life; NO:universal_safe_phrasing eg: "new_chapter_of_adult_life_awaits" >> "I_remember_when_you_came_to_my_4th_grade_whispering_if_I_was_kind_or_strict"

principle: Verbal numbering is oral structuring — in writing use paragraphs or real numbered lists
explanation: X.copy|verbal_numbering: "firstly/secondly/finally" -> oral_structuring_tool; writing: one_paragraph == one_thought; numeric_list ONLY for instructions_or_referenced_items; NO:numbering_unordered_criteria eg: "1._truth 2._testability 3._rigor" >> "— truth — testability — rigor"

principle: Use a vivid single example to establish credibility — one specific true detail outperforms a page of statistics because the brain uses simulation not calculation to judge truth
explanation: X.copy|credibility_detail: Heath: statistical_credibility vs vivid_detail_credibility; vivid_detail: one_unexpected_true_specificity signals_"they_know_this_deeply"; Sinatra_test: if_you_can_do_X_in_hard_conditions_you_can_do_it_anywhere; anti-authority: credibility_from_insider_who_has_lived_it NOT_from_title; NO:page_of_statistics_with_no_story NO:credentials_without_a_vivid_proof_moment; eg: "Subway_lowered_fat_intake_of_customers" >> "Jared_lost_245_lbs_eating_Subway_every_day"

principle: Keep subject and verb close — bureaucratic insertions between them make sentences unreadable
explanation: X.copy|subject_verb_close: bureaucrats_insert_passports+addresses+regulations_between_subject_and_predicate; reader_loses_the_action; fix: core_sentence_first (subject+verb) -> details_in_next_sentences; enumerations_of_conditions -> extract_into_list; NO:mega-sentences_with_buried_predicate eg: "students_who_passed_and_registered_and_living_and_not_earning...may_apply" >> "who_qualifies?_list:_passed/registered/living/not_earning"

principle: Pragmatic headlines must name the specific situation — no wordplay, the more concrete the more the reader feels it's about THEIR problem
explanation: X.copy|pragmatic_headline: direct + specific_situation > clever_wordplay; reader_came_to_solve_problem_fast; add: audience + time + place + method + budget; long_headline_OK_if_every_part_is_useful; NO:playful_headlines_for_problem_solving eg: "dry_and_doesnt_smell" (plumber_article) >> "how_to_find_plumber_on_Avito_if_no_time_for_search"

principle: Open headlines with one of the 22 proven attention triggers — generic headlines are skipped by a brain evolved to filter irrelevant stimuli
explanation: X.copy|power_headline_starters: proven_openers: Free+New+How_to+You+Save+Guarantee+Discover+Proven+Easy+Results+Now+Announcing+Introducing+Secret+Warning+At_last+At_last+Limited+Wanted+Why+Who_else+Which; each_triggers_pattern_interrupt; "How_to"->promise_skill; NO:clever_wordplay_without_trigger; eg: "Our_new_platform" >> "How_to_ship_features_twice_as_fast — without_extra_hires"

principle: Tap the mass desire already flowing in your market — advertising cannot create desire, it can only channel pre-existing desire toward your product
explanation: X.copy|mass_desire_tap: Schwartz: desire_pre-exists_in_market -> copywriter_channels_it NOT creates_it; method: identify_dominant_mass_desire_of_target(financial_security+recognition+health+belonging) -> connect_product_as_the_vehicle; desire_must_be: mass(millions_feel_it)+urgent+already_acknowledged; NO:educating_market_into_wanting_your_product; eg: selling_project_mgmt: don't_explain_features >> tap_"I_want_my_team_to_respect_my_decisions" -> "The_tool_that_makes_you_the_organized_one"

principle: Make readers care about your message by connecting it to something they already care about — self-interest and identity are the two reliable wires to emotion
explanation: X.copy|emotional_connection: Heath: people_don't_care_about_your_message -> connect_to_something_they_already_care_about; two_wires: (1)self-interest: WIIFY(What's_In_It_For_You) -> concrete_personal_benefit (2)identity: this_is_who_you_are_or_want_to_be; Maslow_trap: most_copy_targets_higher_needs_but_concrete_lower_needs_more_reliable; NO:"our_platform_is_important"; eg: "Better_productivity_software" >> "For_the_person_who_keeps_the_team_on_track_when_no_one_else_will"

principle: Show the product in use by a recognizable type of user, not on a white background — context images communicate who the product is for without a single word
explanation: X.copy|lp_hero_image: product_on_white_bg: shows_what_it_is NOT who_it's_for+when_it's_used; context_image: shows_target_user+environment+emotional_state_after_success; formats: (1)screenshot_in_realistic_browser_mockup (2)person_using_product_in_relevant_setting (3)before/after_comparison; NO:stock_photo_of_generic_office_workers; eg: UI_screenshot_white_bg >> designer_at_desk_using_product+satisfied_expression

principle: When list elements outgrow the format — use paragraphs with headings; complex data wants a table
explanation: X.copy|list_limits: bullet==glanceable_homogeneous_items; element_is_whole_paragraph->make_it_paragraph_with_subheading; "here_is_what"_before_list==crutch->fix_intro_sentence; multi-dimensional_data->table; NO:forcing_long_content_into_bullet_format; eg: 3_bullets_each_200_words_long >> 3_subsections_H3_headings+paragraphs

principle: Rankings are useless without reader benefit — state position and explain what it gives them
explanation: X.copy|rankings: "top_20" usually_means_18th -> state_actual_position; position_alone == still_useless; explain_what_it_means_for_reader; NO:vague_bragging eg: "we_are_in_top_20_producers" >> "offices_in_40_cities_=_delivery_anywhere"

principle: Structure success messages with three beats: confirm what happened, reveal what happens next, and match the emotional weight of the moment
explanation: X.copy|success_message_formula: "Done."/"Success" -> confirms_nothing_helps_nothing; formula: (1)CONFIRM: what_just_happened (2)NEXT: what_happens_now (3)CONNECT: emotional_match_to_context; high_stakes(payment)->serious+reassuring; NO:"Success!"_alone; eg: "Saved" >> "Changes_saved — they'll_show_up_immediately"

principle: Find the single core message and cut everything else — a message trying to say three things says nothing, and the curse of knowledge makes experts unable to see what they are burying
explanation: X.copy|commander_intent: Heath: military_concept: commander_gives_ONE_intent_not_10_orders -> soldiers_improvise_toward_it; curse_of_knowledge: expert_cannot_remember_not_knowing -> over-explains -> buries_core; find_core: keep_asking_"what's_the_most_important?"_until_one_thing_remains; lead_with_core_not_caveats; NO:three_headlines_competing_for_attention NO:burying_the_point_in_paragraph_4; eg: "We_offer_quality_service_with_expertise_and_value" >> "Southwest_is_THE_low-fare_airline"

principle: Use a video only if it shows the product working in a real use case — ambient hero videos and talking-head intros have near-zero conversion impact while they add significant page weight
explanation: X.copy|lp_video_strategy: video_types_by_conversion_impact: (1)product_demo_in_context: HIGH (2)customer_testimonial: HIGH (3)explainer_animation: MEDIUM (4)founder_talking: LOW (5)ambient_footage: LOW; video_rules: autoplay_muted_only(no_sound); provide_text_alternative; NO:autoplay_video_with_sound; eg: ambient_drone_footage_hero >> product_demo_video+fallback_static_image

principle: Replace abstract benefits with concrete sensory details — concrete information sticks in memory because the brain encodes experiences, not categories
explanation: X.copy|concreteness_velcro: Heath: memory = Velcro; more_hooks(concrete_details) -> sticks_better; abstract: "high_performance" -> nothing_to_attach_to; concrete: specific+sensory+measurable -> hooks_to_existing_memory; test: "can_I_see/hear/touch_this_in_my_mind?"; NO:abstract_category_words("solution"+"platform"+"performance") NO:benefit_without_specific_number_or_scenario; eg: "improves_team_performance" >> "Engineers_ship_on_Friday_and_stop_dreading_Monday_standup"

principle: Write empty state copy that frames possibility not absence — show what this space will become, not that nothing is here yet
explanation: X.copy|empty_state_copy: "No_items_yet"+"Nothing_here"+"Empty" -> confirms_void; better: describe_potential+give_first_action; formula: [what_space_is_for]+[benefit_when_filled]+[CTA_to_start]; invitation_NOT_absence; NO:"No_projects_yet" NO:"Your_list_is_empty"; eg: "No_projects_yet" >> "Your_projects_live_here — create_your_first_one_to_get_started"; "Inbox_empty" >> "All_clear!_New_messages_will_appear_here"

principle: Place authority signals immediately adjacent to the claim they support — credentials separated from the claim by scroll distance lose their persuasive power
explanation: X.copy|authority_placement: Cialdini: authority_cue -> bypasses_deliberation -> trust_shortcut; proximity_rule: credential_within_same_visual_unit_as_claim; authority_types: (1)earned(degree/cert) (2)third-party(press/awards) (3)data(cited_research+numbers) (4)experience(years+volume); NO:awards_buried_in_footer NO:vague_"experts_say" NO:authority_on_about_page_only; eg: claim_alone >> "Dr._Lee,_Stanford_HCI(2024): this_pattern_lifts_completion_31%" [placed_adjacent_to_form]

principle: The headline makes the opening bid on desire — every line of body copy must expand that bid, never introduce a new promise that was not opened by the headline
explanation: X.copy|headline_opening_bid: headline = first_contract_with_reader; body_copy = series_of_expansions_of_that_promise; new_promise_in_body = broken_contract -> reader_feels_misled; NO:bait-and-switch_headline; eg: headline:"Double_your_close_rate" -> body_expands_HOW+PROOF+WHEN NOT introduces_"also_saves_time" as_new_promise

principle: Structure text around reader needs not company story — ask for whom, what need, how help; segment audiences into separate pages
explanation: X.copy|reader_first: ask: for_whom + what_need + how_help == your_writing_plan; useful_action: why_reader_comes -> what_changes_in_mind -> how_we_get_there; different_audiences == different_pages; one_text_for_all == serves_none; NO:company_story_first NO:one_page_for_everyone eg: "About_us:_founded_in_2005_our_mission..." >> "page_for_homeowners: how_to_choose_insulation + page_for_contractors: bulk_pricing_and_specs"

principle: Replace every vague adjective with a specific number — the brain treats precision as proof and treats vague claims as marketing noise
explanation: X.copy|specificity_credibility: vague==unverifiable -> ignored_as_marketing; specific_number: triggers_"they_measured_it" heuristic -> believable; odd_numbers > round_numbers(round_seems_estimated); specificity_rule: replace adjective->measurement ("fast"->"loads_in_0.8s"; "saves_time"->"saves_4.3h/week"); NO:"significantly_faster" NO:"dramatically_improves" NO:round_100s; eg: "dramatically_reduces_costs" >> "cuts_AWS_bill_by_$1,247/month_on_average"

principle: Deploy fear in four sequential steps — threat named, probability established, consequence made vivid, solution offered — fear without an exit creates paralysis not action
explanation: X.copy|fear_factor_4step: Leventhal_model: (1)name_specific_threat (2)show_high_probability_of_happening (3)vivid_consequences_if_ignored (4)easy_effective_solution_YOU_provide; fear_without_step4 -> denial+freeze NOT action; solution_must_feel_achievable; NO:vague_fear("bad_things_happen") NO:terror_without_escape NO:solution_buried_below_fold; eg: "security_risks"(vague) >> "83%_of_SMBs_hit_ransomware_in_2024->avg_$200k_loss->one_click_blocks_it"

principle: Position the customer as the hero and your brand as the guide — brands that make themselves the hero bore customers who only care about their own story
explanation: X.copy|customer_as_hero: human_brain: protagonist_of_own_story; brand_talking_about_itself==noise; guide_role: Yoda+Gandalf_archetype -> empowers_hero_doesnt_replace; test: replace_"we"_with_"you" -> reads_better == was_wrong; NO:"We_are_a_leading_provider" NO:"Our_innovative_solution" NO:"Our_story_started_in_2012"; eg: "Our_platform_is_revolutionary" >> "You_want_projects_that_never_fall_through_cracks"

principle: List features from the customer's most desired outcome down to operational details — pricing tables read top to bottom and most visitors decide on the first three feature lines
explanation: X.copy|feature_list_order: pricing_table_scan: top_to_bottom; most_decisions: first_3_features; order_rule: (1)core_outcome_feature(what_they_came_for) (2)key_differentiator_between_tiers (3)support_level (4)limits(users+storage) (5)operational_details_last; NO:leading_with_storage_limits NO:alphabetical_feature_ordering NO:operational_items_above_value_items; eg: pricing_table_starting_with_"5GB_storage" >> pricing_table_starting_with_"Unlimited_projects+team_collaboration"

principle: Place the strongest testimonial immediately after the pricing section — the hardest conversion moment is the price reveal, and social proof at that exact point reduces abandonment
explanation: X.copy|testimonial_placement: abandonment_spike: after_price_revealed; strongest_testimonial: most_specific+closest_to_target_buyer+mentions_ROI; placement_priority: (1)immediately_after_pricing (2)adjacent_to_primary_CTA (3)after_problem_section; NO:all_testimonials_in_one_block; eg: testimonials_section_mid_page >> strongest_ROI_testimonial_directly_below_pricing

principle: Big numbers need comparison to land — translate to relatable human scale
explanation: X.copy|number_comparison: abstract_big_number -> meaningless_without_reference; translate_to_familiar_scale; NO:assuming_reader_grasps_magnitude eg: "54.6M_km_to_Mars" >> "light_takes_3_minutes_to_reach_Mars"

principle: Lead with annual pricing as the default and show the savings explicitly — users anchored to annual first see monthly as a premium upgrade, not the other way around
explanation: X.copy|annual_monthly_toggle: toggle_default_matters: monthly_default -> annual_feels_expensive_commitment; annual_default -> monthly_feels_convenient_but_costlier; annual_savings_display: show_both_% ("Save_20%") AND_$ saved; billing_label: "per_month,_billed_annually"_not_"$X/year"; toggle_placement: above_pricing_cards; NO:monthly_as_default_toggle NO:annual_savings_hidden_or_only_in_fine_print; eg: monthly_default_toggle >> annual_default+"Save_20%"_badge_on_toggle

principle: Place all assistive microcopy above or to the right of its control — instructions that appear after an action arrive too late and cause errors
explanation: X.copy|accessible_placement: read_order: top_to_bottom_left_to_right; correct_sequence: Label -> Assistive_text -> Control_element; help_AFTER_control -> user_acts_before_reading -> error; critical_guidance_must_be_static NOT tooltip_only; NO:helper_text_below_input NO:instruction_after_submit_button NO:critical_info_in_hover_tooltip_only; eg: "First_name [input] * Required" >> "* Required — First_name [input]"; "[checkbox] I agree to terms" >> "By_checking_you_agree_to_terms [checkbox]"

principle: Establish guide credibility with empathy and authority together — empathy without proof sounds unqualified, authority without empathy sounds cold
explanation: X.copy|guide_empathy_authority: empathy_alone: well-meaning_but_unqualified; authority_alone: cold+bureaucratic; empathy_signal: naming_the_internal_emotion_customer_feels; authority_signal: stats+case_studies+years+awards; place_both: about_section+hero_subheadline; NO:credentials_without_empathy NO:sympathy_without_proof; eg: "Award-winning_since_2010" >> "We_know_the_panic_of_a_burst_pipe — we've_fixed_5000_of_them"

principle: Indefinite hedges hide missing info — delete them, reader needs orientation not false precision
explanation: X.copy|indefinite_hedges: "more_than/about/approximately/some" -> noise; reader_needs_ballpark NOT precision_disclaimers; NO:hedging_every_number; if_legally_needed -> add_date_instead eg: "more_than_20000_clients" >> "20_thousand_clients"

principle: Open with an unexpected gap in the reader's knowledge — curiosity is the feeling of a hole, and the brain cannot rest until the hole is filled
explanation: X.copy|curiosity_gap: Heath+Loewenstein: curiosity = gap_between_what_I_know + what_I_want_to_know; method: (1)identify_assumption_audience_holds (2)violate_it (3)show_the_gap (4)offer_to_fill_it; teaser_formula: "You_think_X... but_actually_Y" -> creates_gap -> "here's_why"; NO:leading_with_conclusion(no_gap); eg: "5_tips_for_email" >> "The_word_most_email_marketers_never_use — and_why_it_doubles_replies"

principle: Build liking through shared struggle not flattery — visitors connect to brands that confess the same pain they felt, not to brands that compliment them
explanation: X.copy|liking_shared_struggle: Cialdini: we_favor_those_similar_to_us+those_who_understand_us; shared_struggle_technique: reveal_you_had_same_problem -> you_built_solution_for_yourself; similarity_signals: same_role+same_pain+same_failed_attempts; NO:artificial_familiarity("Hey_friend!") NO:compliment_opener("You're_smart_for_being_here") NO:overly_polished_founder_story; eg: "We_are_passionate_about_productivity" >> "We_burned_$40k_on_tools_that_didn't_fit — so_we_built_this_instead"

principle: Jargon and bureaucratic abstractions deliberately hide problems — simplify to expose the real situation and force accountability
explanation: X.copy|jargon_hides: complex_synonyms + bureaucratic_abstractions == shield_from_accountability; "optimization_of_organizational_processes" == nobody_knows_what_happened; simplify -> expose_truth; same_word_can_be_jargon_or_precise_term: keep_when_real_discipline_cut_when_decoration; NO:inflated_language_to_sound_important eg: "implementation_of_corrective_measures_regarding_resource_allocation" >> "we_fired_two_people_and_cut_the_budget"

principle: Borrow credibility from symbols and institutions your audience already trusts — transfer technique shortcuts years of trust-building in a single visual
explanation: X.copy|transfer_technique: borrowed_credibility: associate_product_with_trusted_symbol/institution->trust_transfers; types: (1)logos: "as_seen_in_Forbes/NYT/TechCrunch" (2)certification_badges: SOC2+HIPAA+ISO (3)scientific_backing: "Stanford_research" (4)celebrity/expert_endorsement; mechanism: classical_conditioning->adjacent_trust; NO:fake_logos; eg: no_social_proof >> "Trusted_by_50k_teams [G2_4.9][SOC2][Forbes_logo]" near_primary_CTA

principle: Escalate your claim type as the market matures — direct product claims only work when your category is new, a saturated market demands a novel mechanism or identity frame
explanation: X.copy|sophistication_5stages: (1)FIRST_IN_MARKET: direct_claim_works("lose_weight_fast") (2)COMPETITORS_COPY: bigger_claim("lose_30lbs_in_30_days") (3)CLAIMS_EXHAUSTED: introduce_mechanism("HGH-activating_formula") (4)MECHANISMS_COPIED: elaborate_mechanism("7-stage_thermogenic_cascade") (5)ALL_EXHAUSTED: identification+sensation("for_women_over_40_who_gave_up"); NO:direct_claim_in_stage4+_market; eg: crowded_CRM: "best_CRM" >> "The_only_CRM_built_around_how_sales_reps_actually_think"

principle: Parentheses visually say skip me — extract important content or delete unimportant
explanation: X.copy|parentheses: content_in_brackets -> visually_demoted; important? -> extract_to_main_text; unimportant? -> delete; OK_only_for_citations_at_sentence_end; NO:hiding_key_info_in_brackets eg: "Vasily_(factory_director)_greeted_us" >> "factory_director_Vasily_greeted_us"

principle: Proactive care: don't just inform — help solve the problem with addresses, forms, next steps
explanation: X.copy|proactive_care: basic: "apply_at_local_office"; better: add_address+hours+phone; best: attach_form+step-by-step+link; every_step_toward_helping == more_trust; good_text_costs_same_as_bad -> templateable; NO:"informing_is_our_job_rest_is_theirs" eg: "contact_your_local_UMVD" >> "your_UMVD:_address_X_hours_Y_room_Z_book_online_at_link"

principle: Three mind constraints for sentences: limited memory, image-based thinking, scanning eyes
explanation: X.copy|three_constraints: 1_working_memory_limited -> dont_overload_sentences; 2_we_think_in_images -> help_reader_paint_picture_from_what_they_know; 3_eyes_scan_for_key_info -> dont_hide_important_in_the_middle; NO:abstract_info_dumps NO:burying_the_lead eg: "abstract_noun_pile" >> "person_does_action_reader_can_picture"

principle: Content drives structure, never the reverse — don't force material into a template; report needs results first, not a story
explanation: X.copy|content_over_template: NO:blind_adherence_to_formula (pyramid/storytelling/modules); if_structure_doesnt_fit_content -> change_structure; story != universal_wrapper; boss_wants_results_first NOT backstory; module_structure_should_follow_from_what_you_say; NO:"lets_use_storytelling_for_everything" eg: conference_report_as_story >> boss_wants: "results:_X_deals_Y_revenue_Z%_of_plan._Details_below"

principle: Read every interface label and message aloud — if you would never say it in conversation, rewrite it until a real person would
explanation: X.copy|conversational_test: read_UI_copy_aloud -> sounds_robotic/legal/formal -> rewrite; non_conversational_symptoms: passive_voice+noun_stacks+legal_qualifiers+third_person_system_refs; conversational_markers: contractions+first+second_person+active_voice+natural_rhythm; NO:"Your_submission_has_been_received"; eg: "Your_request_has_been_submitted_for_processing" >> "We_got_it!_We'll_be_in_touch_shortly"

principle: Structure an About page as origin story, mission, team, and single CTA — a company history dump with no narrative arc fails to build the human connection that About pages exist to create
explanation: X.copy|about_page_structure: company_history_dump: no_emotional_connection+no_action; narrative_arc: (1)origin_story: what_problem_we_personally_faced (2)mission: one_sentence_what_we_exist_to_do (3)team: real_humans_with_faces+short_human_bios (4)CTA: work_with_us+contact; tone: first_person_plural_"we"; NO:list_of_founding_years_and_office_locations NO:stock_photo_team_without_names; eg: boring_history_tab >> founder_story+mission_statement+team_grid+"Work_with_us"_CTA

principle: Dense indefinite corporate text means saying everything while committing to nothing — replace with named services and clients
explanation: X.copy|corporate_fog: "wide_range/many_years/leading_provider/many_directions" == company_wants_to_brag_but_fears_commitment; NO:compress_everything_into_one_vague_paragraph; fix: name_services + list_clients + cite_numbers eg: "wide_range_of_services_for_various_industries" >> "automate_accounting_warehouse_logistics_for_140_companies"

principle: Reframe annual price as a daily or weekly cost — $365/year triggers loss aversion, $1/day triggers comparison to coffee and feels negligible
explanation: X.copy|price_framing_small: large_number_anchor: $365_feels_expensive; equivalent_small: $1/day_feels_trivial; comparison_frame: "less_than_your_daily_coffee"; Weber-Fechner: people_evaluate_proportionally_not_absolutely; use_alongside_real_price: "$365/year_($1/day)"; best_for: mid-market_B2C_and_prosumer; NOT_for: enterprise(procurement_doesn't_think_in_daily_coffee); NO:showing_only_annual_total NO:comparison_to_irrelevant_items; eg: "$29/month" >> "$29/month — less_than_$1/day"

principle: State verbs hide real actions — restore them, but don't force action where there's genuinely none
explanation: X.copy|hidden_actions: two_patterns: state_verbs ("is/exists/appears") + verbal_nouns ("perform/conduct/implement"+noun); both_hide_real_actions; restore: find_hidden_verb -> replace; NO:mechanical_replacement_when_no_action_exists eg: "CRM_ensures_sales_growth" >> "after_CRM_managers_sell_30%_more"; "company_performs_delivery" >> "company_delivers"; "70%_warehouses_in_Moscow" -> OK_as_is

principle: Structure every testimonial with five elements: problem before, specific result, time frame, name and role, photo — testimonials missing any element lose credibility
explanation: X.copy|testimonial_formula: weak_testimonial: "Great_product!_-_John"(unverifiable); strong_5elements: (1)problem_before (2)specific_quantified_result (3)timeframe (4)full_name+role+company (5)photo_or_video; each_element_adds_verification_signal; photo: +34%_trust(Nielson); specifics > superlatives; NO:anonymous NO:no_before-state NO:vague_outcome NO:photo_omitted; eg: "Love_it!"-Jane >> "Cut_onboarding_from_8h_to_45min_in_3_weeks" — Jane Park, Head of CS, Acme Corp [photo]

principle: Don't project your wounds onto new readers — aggressive disclaimers about past bad clients lecture innocent people
explanation: X.copy|wound_projection: designer_burned_by_"need_it_yesterday" -> puts_angry_disclaimer_on_site; reader_who_hasnt_done_anything -> gets_lectured_at_first_meeting; your_past_pain != readers_question; NO:preemptive_aggression_from_bad_experiences; address_real_reader_questions_not_your_trauma eg: "I_dont_work_with_rush_orders_dont_even_ask" >> "typical_timeline:_one_week._For_faster_options_lets_discuss"

principle: Amplifiers weaken what follows — delete without loss; blind-name test reveals empty text
explanation: X.copy|amplifiers: "absolutely/extremely/truly/incredibly" -> stacked_on_evaluation == desperation_not_conviction; delete_amplifier -> meaning_intact; blind_test: cover_company_name -> if_text_fits_any_company == empty; NO:amplifying_emptiness eg: "absolutely_free" >> "free"; "maximally_quality_repair" >> "repair_with_guarantee"

principle: Write the page headline to match the ad or search query that brought the visitor — message mismatch between ad and landing page is the single largest source of landing page abandonment
explanation: X.copy|message_match: ad_to_lp_mismatch: visitor_sees_ad_promise -> clicks -> lands_on_different_message -> feels_misled -> bounces; match_rule: headline_on_LP_uses_same_language+same_promise_as_the_ad; dynamic_text_replacement: swap_headline_based_on_UTM_source; NO:generic_homepage_headline_for_all_traffic; eg: ad_"Best_CRM_for_plumbers"+LP_headline_"CRM_software" >> LP_headline_"CRM_built_for_plumbing_businesses"

principle: Introduction is not a separate task — lead with your strongest material; throat-clearing openings mean you have nothing to say yet
explanation: X.copy|intro_is_content: NO:writing_intro_as_separate_task; intro == first_piece_of_your_strongest_material; if_you_have_strong_facts -> lead_with_them; strong_experience -> lead_with_it; strong_solution -> give_it_immediately; weak_openings (metaphor/generalization/"everyone_knows"/season) == author_has_nothing_to_say_yet eg: "everyone_knows_not_sleeping_is_bad" >> "healthy_adult_falls_asleep_in_10-20min._Over_30min_=_problem"

principle: Map reassurance copy against the seven universal user concerns — each anxiety type needs specific inline text placed at the exact moment of hesitation
explanation: X.copy|seven_concerns: (1)email:"One_email/month,_unsubscribe_anytime" (2)data:explain_why+how (3)social_login:"Won't_post" (4)payment:"256-bit_encryption" (5)trial:"No_credit_card" (6)settings:"Change_later" (7)download:"No_extras"; place_INLINE_at_exact_moment; NO:generic_trust_badge_covering_all; eg: email_field_no_context >> email_field+"One_email/month"_below

principle: Find real actors in impersonal sentences — but skip when the actor is obvious or irrelevant
explanation: X.copy|find_actor: "fines_are_applied" -> who_fines? -> "company_fines_employees"; BUT obvious_actor == noise; NO:adding_self-evident_performers eg: "mechanics_installed_the_engine" >> pointless_if_only_mechanics_can; better: "the_engine_was_5_liters" -> hero_is_engine_not_mechanic

principle: Strip jargon by asking whether a ten-year-old could understand your message — if they could not, the words are hiding the idea not expressing it
explanation: X.copy|jargon_strip: Heath_curse_of_knowledge: jargon = shorthand_experts_use; outsider_hears_jargon -> cannot_build_mental_model -> disengages; test: "could_a_10-year-old_understand_this_sentence?"; NO:feature_names_as_benefits; eg: "cloud-native_microservices_orchestration" >> "each_part_of_the_app_scales_on_its_own_so_one_bug_can't_crash_everything"

principle: Same event becomes a different story depending on headline focus — choosing the angle IS the editorial act
explanation: X.copy|headline_angle: same_event -> 5_headlines -> 5_stories; headline_selects: hero OR cost OR scandal OR benefit; closing_paragraphs_also_shape_meaning; NO:thinking_headlines_are_objective eg: Kaluga_brand: "new_tourism_brand_2026" vs "10M_for_Moscow_designers" vs "scandalous_designer_at_apartment_price" == same_facts_different_stories

principle: The 'therefore' test: any company claim must continue with concrete action or reader benefit — values without 'therefore' are empty words
explanation: X.copy|therefore_test: "we_value_X" -> must_continue: "THEREFORE_we_do_Y_and_refuse_Z"; also: "we_have_X" -> "THIS_MEANS_for_you:_Y"; mission_without_concrete_action == decoration; real_values: state_belief + prove_with_action + show_what_you_refuse; NO:abstract_mission_anyone_could_copy eg: "we_value_quality" >> "we_value_quality_THEREFORE_24/7_support_call_us_midnight_new_years_eve"; "no_tobacco_clients_because_we_want_ads_to_make_world_better"

principle: Use awareness level to choose your entry point and sophistication level to choose your claim type — mismatching either makes copy invisible regardless of craft quality
explanation: X.copy|awareness_sophistication_matrix: awareness(5_levels:unaware->most_aware)->sets_entry_point(problem-lead_vs_deal-lead); sophistication(5_stages:fresh->exhausted)->sets_claim_type(direct_vs_mechanism_vs_story); NO:fresh_direct_claim_in_exhausted_market; eg: B2B_SaaS:"Meet_Acme_CRM"(fresh_claim_saturated_market) >> "Cut_sales_cycle_30%"(result-lead_for_sophisticated_buyer)

principle: Write body copy as a chain of expanding commitments — each sentence must deepen the desire opened by the previous, every sideways pivot breaks the chain and loses the reader
explanation: X.copy|expanding_commitment_chain: Schwartz: copy = series_of_micro-yeses; each_sentence: deepens+confirms+expands_previous_commitment; pivot_to_new_idea = chain_break -> reader_disengages; NO:topic_hopping_in_body_paragraphs; eg: paragraph_on_speed->pivot_to_pricing >> paragraph_on_speed->HOW_fast->PROOF->WHO_benefited

principle: Add a money-back guarantee badge adjacent to every primary pricing CTA — risk reversal converts fence-sitters who want the product but fear making a mistake
explanation: X.copy|risk_reversal: buyer_hesitation: "what_if_it_doesn't_work?" = primary_objection_at_purchase; risk_reversal: remove_downside_risk -> lower_decision_barrier; types: (1)30/60-day_money_back_guarantee (2)free_trial_no_credit_card (3)cancel_anytime; NO:guarantee_buried_in_footer; eg: CTA_button_alone >> CTA_button+"30-day_money-back_guarantee"_below

principle: Each evaluation hides a story the author was too lazy to tell — the adjective is a shortcut past real work
explanation: X.copy|eval_as_shortcut: adjective == compressed_story_author_skipped; "eco-friendly" hides sourcing + manufacturing + certification; author_thinks_one_word == job_done; NO:false_sense_of_completed_work; unpack_each_claim_into_its_story eg: "eco-friendly_floors" >> "wood_from_sustainable_forests_+_natural_resin_coatings_safe_for_nurseries"

principle: Falsehood is the worst tonal crime — readers tolerate ads but hate hidden intentions; be direct about what you want
explanation: X.copy|anti_falsehood: readers_accept: ads + sales + self-promotion; readers_reject: hidden_commercial_intent + fake_friendliness + manufactured_characters; falsehood == mismatch_between_stated_and_real_motive; fix: state_intentions_openly + communicate_as_equals; NO:disguising_sales_as_caring NO:fake_brand_mascots eg: "you_asked_about_loafers_heres_my_secret_favorite_brand_(ad)" >> "ad_time:_my_favorite_shoe_brand_from_Ryazan._I_own_4_pairs._Discount_for_readers"

principle: Examples force reader to mentally rehearse your experience — without them, conclusions don't stick even if understood
explanation: X.copy|examples_as_rehearsal: author_has_years_of_experience -> compresses_into_conclusion; reader_reads_conclusion_but_cant_apply: holds_phone_with_diet_article + reaches_for_sweet_yogurt; example_forces_mental_practice: reader_imagines_SPECIFIC_situation -> connects_abstract_to_concrete; NO:conclusions_without_examples; finding_good_examples == the_actual_work eg: "avoid_fast_carbs" >> "avoid_fast_carbs:_pastries_sandwiches_sweet_drinks_even_fruit_yogurts"

principle: Lead copy with the desire, name the mechanism that satisfies it, then introduce the product as the best vehicle for that mechanism — opening with the product reverses the psychological sequence
explanation: X.copy|desire_mechanism_product: Schwartz_sequence: desire(emotional_want) -> mechanism(process_that_delivers_result) -> product(best_instance_of_mechanism); reader_cares_about_desire_first NOT product_name; mechanism: the_ingredient_or_process_bridging_desire_to_product; NO:product_first_copy("introducing_X_software") NO:desire_with_no_mechanism(empty_promise); eg: "Our_AI_writes_emails" >> "Get_replies_in_24h(desire) -> neural_send-time_optimization(mechanism) -> powered_by_Reachly(product)"

principle: Reading is constructing a mental movie — if the reader can't picture it, the sentence is fog
explanation: X.copy|mental_movie: reader_decodes_words -> mental_images like_watching_film; if_image_forms -> understood; if_not -> fog; too_much_fog -> reader_skips_paragraphs/quits; NO:assuming_reader_decodes_abstractions_automatically eg: "possible_hyperinflation_like_Venezuela" -> works_if_reader_knows_Venezuela_2018 >> fails_if_they_dont

principle: One hero and one plot per sentence — if the sentence doesn't know what it's about, split it
explanation: X.copy|one_hero: sentence_must_have_one_subject; cramming_multiple_actors+events -> unreadable; ask "who_is_the_hero?"; split_rest_into_next_sentences; includes_compound_clauses_with_no_link; NO:multi-plot_sentences eg: "man_slipped_breaking_car_window_with_bottle_nearly_hitting_child" >> "man_slipped._Bottle_hit_car._Glass_flew_toward_child"

principle: Every sentence about your brand's history or values is noise to a customer scanning for their own survival — filter all copy through 'does this help the customer win?'
explanation: X.copy|noise_vs_signal: customer_brain: scans_for_own_survival_story; brand_talking_about_itself -> "not_relevant" -> ignored; signal: how_customer_life_improves; noise: company_history+mission_statement+award_lists+"we_are_passionate"; filter_test: "does_this_sentence_show_customer_how_life_improves?" NO->cut; NO:"Founded_2003_we_believe_in_quality" NO:mission_statement_as_homepage_headline; eg: "Values-driven_company_committed_to_excellence" >> "You_get_clean_code_delivered_on_time"

principle: Corporate clichés feel safe but say nothing — replace with specific plans, products, departments
explanation: X.copy|corporate_cliche: "ambitious_goals/team_of_professionals/key_to_success" -> safe_because_everyone_uses_them + dead_because_they_say_nothing; replace_with_specifics: what_product + which_market + which_departments; NO:abstract_motivation eg: "expanding_sales_geography" >> "new_building_mixes_for_Far_East_cities_where_we_werent_present"

principle: Paint success as a specific believable scene not a vague promise — customers need to see themselves after the transformation to justify the purchase
explanation: X.copy|vivid_success: vague:"you'll_be_happier"(unbelievable); specific: sensory+concrete+plausible; 3_types: (1)STATUS: others_recognize_achievement (2)COMPLETENESS: recover_what_was_lost(peace/sleep/trust) (3)SELF_REALIZATION: see_yourself_as_capable; formula: name_what_life_looks+sounds+feels_like_after; NO:abstract_benefits NO:hyperbole NO:"peace_of_mind"_alone; eg: "you'll_feel_better" >> "Kids_eat_vegetables._Dinner_is_peaceful._You_feel_like_you're_parenting,_not_surviving."

principle: Build every website header with four elements: what you offer, who benefits, how life improves, and one direct CTA — visitors decide in 5 seconds whether to stay
explanation: X.copy|sb7_header_formula: above_fold_must_have: (1)headline:what_you_offer(clear_NOT_clever) (2)subheadline:who_benefits+outcome (3)image:customer_succeeding_NOT_product_photo (4)CTA:direct_action_verb; visitor_cant_answer_what_you_offer_in_5sec->leaves; NO:clever_tagline_without_clear_offer NO:product_photo_instead_of_success_photo NO:no_CTA_above_fold; eg: "Innovating_the_Future"+product_photo >> "A_CRM_your_team_will_actually_use"+team_winning_photo+"Start_Free_Trial"

principle: Structure a homepage as a progressive commitment sequence: problem recognition, solution glimpse, social proof, feature depth, final CTA — visitors need to see each layer before they trust the next
explanation: X.copy|homepage_sequence: feature_dump_homepage: no_narrative -> visitor_unsure_if_product_is_for_them; progressive_commitment: (1)hero: WHO_this_is_for+WHAT_they_get (2)problem: name_the_pain (3)solution: how_it_works (4)proof: logos+testimonials (5)features: details_for_interested (6)final_CTA: for_those_who_scrolled_all_the_way; SAME_structure_as_landing_page_but_less_aggressive; NO:features_in_hero_section; eg: product_feature_grid_homepage >> problem+solution+proof+features+CTA_sequence

principle: Use placeholder text only where it adds value a label cannot — eye-tracking shows users scan for empty fields and skip inputs that look already filled
explanation: X.copy|placeholder_scanning: eyetracking: users_scan_for_empty_fields; field_with_placeholder_text == looks_non-empty -> user_skips_or_takes_longer_to_locate; overuse_of_placeholders -> slower_form_completion + missed_fields; NO:placeholder_in_every_field_by_default; eg: all_fields_have_example_placeholders >> empty_fields_with_clear_labels + placeholder_only_in_search_and_open-ended_fields

principle: Abstractions compress experience the reader can't unpack — pair the label with concrete examples
explanation: X.copy|unpack_abstraction: author_saw_7_people_working -> compressed_to_"seamless_logistics"; reader_has_no_images_behind_the_label; fix: keep_abstract_label + add_concrete_scenes; NO:abstraction_alone eg: "seamless_logistics" >> "seamless_logistics:_our_staff_builds_routes_negotiates_carriers_handles_customs_—_you_just_name_the_address"

principle: Answer the five buyer objections inline before the FAQ — price too high, doesn't work for my case, takes too long, too risky, and don't trust the brand are the dominant reasons people don't buy
explanation: X.copy|objection_inline: five_universal_objections: (1)price_too_high->ROI_proof+payment_options (2)won't_work_for_me->specific_use_case_proof (3)too_complex/slow->setup_time_data (4)too_risky->guarantee+no_credit_card (5)don't_trust_brand->authority_signals+social_proof; inline_format: two-column_objection+answer OR_FAQ_block before_bottom_CTA; NO:FAQ_only_with_product_questions NO:leaving_objections_for_sales_call; eg: contact_sales_CTA_only >> inline_objection_block+risk_reversal+CTA

principle: Make social proof specific and situational — generic praise is ignored, but proof that matches the visitor's exact role and situation cuts through skepticism
explanation: X.copy|social_proof_specificity: Cialdini: others_choices_reduce_uncertainty -> safety_heuristic; proof_power_order: video > photo+name+role > name_only > anonymous; specificity_rule: outcome+timeframe+role+company = credible; situational_match: show_proof_from_someone_like_the_visitor; NO:"businesses_love_us" NO:anonymous_testimonials NO:aggregate_star_rating_only; eg: "Trusted_by_thousands" >> "3,247_SaaS_teams_use_this — Acme_CTO:_'saved_us_$180k_in_year_1'" [photo+title]

principle: Redirect thank-you pages to the next logical action instead of ending the journey — a 'Thank you, we'll be in touch' page with nothing to do is a missed conversion
explanation: X.copy|thankyou_page: bare_thank_you: journey_ends -> lead_cools; next_action_options: (1)high_value_content_download (2)book_a_call (3)join_community (4)follow_on_social (5)share_referral_link; match_to_conversion_context: lead_magnet_download -> offer_consultation; NO:thank_you_page_with_no_action; eg: "Thanks!_We'll_be_in_touch." >> "Your_report_is_on_the_way._While_you_wait,_book_a_30min_strategy_call."

principle: Show a three-step process below every CTA — customers abandon not because they dislike you but because they cannot picture what happens after they click
explanation: X.copy|three_step_plan: hesitation=="I_don't_know_what_happens_next"; plan_removes_ambiguity+risk; structure: 3_steps_max; two_types: (1)process_plan: what_WE_do_after_CTA (2)agreement_plan: removes_objection(no_upfront_fee+cancel_anytime); place_directly_below_primary_CTA; NO:5+_steps(analysis_paralysis) NO:no_plan_at_all NO:vague_steps; eg: no_next_steps >> "1.Schedule_call 2.We_fix_it 3.You_relax"

principle: Stop-word is attention flag not ban: same word essential or garbage depending on context
explanation: X.copy|context_flag: stop_word == signal "check_this_spot" NOT ban_list; word_carries_meaning? -> keep; word_is_filler? -> cut; NO:mechanical_deletion_by_checklist; author_decides eg: "for_example"_before_real_example -> essential; "for_example"_in_hedge_chain -> noise

principle: Position the free plan as a gateway not a goal — free plan users who see what they cannot do convert to paid, free plan users who see all they can do never upgrade
explanation: X.copy|freemium_gate_strategy: free_plan_purpose: convert_to_paid NOT serve_indefinitely; gate_strategy: free_plan includes_core_value(proves_product_works) + gates_growth_features(team+integrations+advanced_reports); upgrade_trigger: show_locked_features_prominently_when_user_hits_them; NO:free_plan_so_generous_no_reason_to_upgrade; eg: free_plan_with_all_features_limited_to_5 >> free_unlimited+gate_team_features

principle: Position the product as gateway to the identity the customer wants to become — people buy the self-image, the product is just the vehicle
explanation: X.copy|ego_morphing: customers_buy: who_they_will_BE_not_what_product_does; identity_shift: "I_am_the_kind_of_person_who..."; technique: name_the_aspired_identity+show_product_as_the_bridge; mirror_customer_self-concept_in_copy; aspirational_identity > problem_language_in_high-ticket; NO:product_as_destination NO:feature_list_without_identity_frame; eg: "project_management_software" >> "For_founders_who_ship_while_everyone_else_plans"

principle: Structure every landing page in eight sequential sections: hook, problem, solution, social proof, features, objections, CTA cluster, and FAQ — visitors who cannot find a section they need leave to find it elsewhere
explanation: X.copy|lp_section_sequence: (1)HOOK: headline+subhead+CTA_above_fold (2)PROBLEM: name_the_pain_reader_recognizes (3)SOLUTION: product_as_the_fix+mechanism (4)SOCIAL_PROOF: logos+testimonials+metrics (5)FEATURES: benefits_not_specs (6)OBJECTIONS: FAQ_inline_or_objection_block (7)CTA_CLUSTER: repeat_CTA+guarantee+risk_reversal (8)FAQ: bottom_catch_all; order_matters: hook_first_always; NO:features_before_social_proof; eg: feature_dump_page >> 8-section_narrative_flow

principle: Open every blog post with the reader's problem or question, not with context-setting — writers who establish background before stating the point lose readers in the first paragraph
explanation: X.copy|article_opening: context_first: "Since_the_dawn_of_time..." -> reader_leaves; reader_problem_first: state_problem_reader_recognizes -> hook_landed; structure: (1)hook: problem_or_counterintuitive_claim (2)promise: what_reader_will_know_by_end (3)evidence: one_proof_of_claim (4)body: organized_by_reader_questions; NO:throat_clearing_intro; eg: "Marketing_has_many_definitions" >> "Most_landing_pages_fail_because_of_one_fixable_mistake"

principle: Match your headline entry point to the customer's awareness level — leading with the product name to an unaware audience is the most expensive mistake in advertising
explanation: X.copy|awareness_5levels: (1)MOST_AWARE: knows+uses_product->headline=price/deal (2)PRODUCT_AWARE: knows_product_not_bought->headline=differentiation (3)SOLUTION_AWARE: knows_solution_type_not_brand->headline=mechanism (4)PROBLEM_AWARE: feels_pain_no_solution_yet->headline=name_pain_first (5)COMPLETELY_UNAWARE: no_felt_pain->headline=story+desire+identification; eg: SaaS_cold_ad: "Introducing_OurCRM" >> "Still_losing_deals_because_your_notes_are_scattered?"

principle: Show the number of customers on each plan as social proof — per-plan counts anchor the 'most popular' tier with real evidence rather than a marketing badge
explanation: X.copy|per_plan_social_proof: "most_popular"_badge: can_feel_arbitrary+marketing; real_count: "Chosen_by_2,400_teams"_on_pro_plan = verifiable_evidence; variant: logos_of_known_customers_under_enterprise_tier; per_plan_testimonial: one_specific_quote_matching_plan_type("Solo" testimonial_under_basic; "team" testimonial_under_pro); NO:most_popular_badge_without_any_evidence NO:generic_testimonials_not_matched_to_plan; eg: "Most_popular"_badge >> "Most_popular — used_by_3,200_teams" [logo_strip]

principle: Lists need parallel structure and reader's search scenario — start each element from what the reader is looking for
explanation: X.copy|list_design: elements_must_have_same_structure + same_field_order; build_from_reader_perspective: reader_knows_WHO_they_are -> match_to_tariff; NO:author_taxonomy_first; uneven_element == structural_break -> extract_the_outlier; repeated_info_across_all_elements -> extract_above eg: "child_tariff._For_kids_under_12" >> "kids_under_12_—_child_tariff"

principle: Round big numbers and convert units — do the math for the reader
explanation: X.copy|big_numbers: reader_skips_after_3_digits; round_to_meaningful_precision + convert_units; precise_numbers ONLY for accountants/scientists; NO:raw_multidigit_dumps eg: "10_543_768_rubles" >> "10.5M_rubles"; "12_698_341_kg" >> "12.7_thousand_tons"

principle: Lead with genuine free value before any ask — reciprocity is automatic and pre-wired, but only triggers when the gift feels valuable and unconditional
explanation: X.copy|reciprocity_trigger: Cialdini: receiving_creates_obligation_debt -> repayment_urge; gift_must_feel: valuable+unexpected+personalized(NOT_generic_freebie); web_pattern: lead_with_free_resource_BEFORE_email_capture; timing: give_first -> ask_second; perceived_value > actual_cost; NO:generic_whitepaper_as_"gift" NO:gift_with_immediate_hard_sell NO:opt-in_before_value; eg: "Download_our_whitepaper"(gated) >> "Get_47-point_checklist — no_email_required. Most_agencies_miss_#23"

principle: Use tab bars for top-level context switching and navigation controllers for hierarchical drilling — mixing the two patterns creates unresolvable navigational confusion
explanation: X.mobile|ios_nav_model: tab_bar: switches_between_independent_sections(Home/Search/Profile); each_tab_has_own_nav_stack; nav_controller: push_into_detail -> pop_back; mix_antipattern: tab_that_pushes_into_another_tab_content; rule: tapping_tab_returns_to_root_of_that_tab(double-tap); max_tabs: 5(use_More_for_overflow); NO:nav_push_that_switches_tab NO:tabs_within_pushed_detail_screen; eg: settings_pushed_from_home_tab >> settings_as_its_own_tab_with_own_nav_stack

principle: Design loading states with skeleton screens not spinner overlays — skeleton screens preserve layout structure and feel faster even at identical load times
explanation: X.mobile|skeleton_screens: spinner_overlay: blocks_entire_UI -> perceived_freeze; skeleton: shows_layout_with_placeholder_shapes -> brain_predicts_content_before_it_arrives; implementation: use_shimmer_animation_on_gray_placeholder_shapes; NO:full_screen_activity_indicator_for_content_loads; eg: UIActivityIndicatorView_full_screen >> skeleton_cells_with_shimmer

principle: Use sheets for modal presentations on iOS — center-screen dialogs are a desktop pattern and conflict with thumb-based dismissal via swipe-down
explanation: X.mobile|ios_sheets: UIAlertController: only_for_brief_alerts(1-2_actions); full_modal_content: UISheetPresentationController(.medium OR .large detent); SwiftUI: .sheet()+.presentationDetents([.medium,.large]); dismiss: swipe_down OR explicit_button; medium_detent: shows_partial_content(~50%_screen); full_detent: full_screen_form/flow; NO:custom_center_dialog_overlays NO:bottom_sheet_with_no_drag_indicator; eg: UIViewController_presented_fullscreen >> UISheetPresentationController_medium_detent

principle: Apply M3 shape scale to communicate component personality — smaller radius reads as professional, larger radius reads as expressive and friendly
explanation: X.mobile|m3_shape_scale: shape_tokens: none(0dp)+extraSmall(4dp)+small(8dp)+medium(12dp)+large(16dp)+extraLarge(28dp)+full(50%); component_defaults: TextButton=full+Card=medium+Dialog=extraLarge+BottomSheet=extraLarge+Chip=small; customizing: override_MaterialTheme.shapes not hardcode_per_component; brand_feel: 0dp=strict/technical, full_radius=playful; NO:hardcoded_cornerRadius_in_each_composable; eg: RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)_per_composable >> MaterialTheme.shapes.small_applied_globally

principle: Support Dynamic Type for all text in your app — users who set Accessibility Large Text sizes need content to scale or the app is unusable
explanation: X.mobile|dynamic_type: fixed_font_sizes -> inaccessible_for_large_text_users(up_to_310%_scale); implementation: semantic_text_styles(UIFont.TextStyle.body+headline+caption) NOT fixed_pt_sizes; SwiftUI: .font(.body)+.dynamicTypeSize(.xSmall..._.accessibility5); test: run_at_XXXLarge_accessibility_size; NO:hardcoded_UIFont.systemFont(ofSize:14) NO:truncating_all_text_at_max_size; eg: UIFont.systemFont(ofSize:16) >> UIFont.preferredFont(forTextStyle:.body)

principle: Mobile users are interrupted and multitasking — design for one-handed thumb reach, minimal input, and instant value on launch; never assume a user's full attention
explanation: X.mobile|limited_attention: context: walking+riding+talking+meeting while_using_phone; implications: minimize_required_input+reduce_steps_to_goal+support_interruption_recovery(restore_state)+one-handed_thumb_reach_for_primary_actions; design_for: Microbreaks(2_min)+immediate_value+no_required_setup_before_first_value; NO:desktop_feature_parity_ported_directly_to_mobile NO:long_forms_requiring_sustained_attention; eg: 7-step_mobile_checkout >> 3-step+autofill -> completion_rate_doubles

principle: Use elevation via surface tint not drop shadows in Material Design 3 — M3 communicates elevation by blending primary color into the surface, not by casting shadows
explanation: X.mobile|m3_elevation: M2_shadows: box_shadows_for_depth; M3_elevation: surface_tint_color(primary_overlaid_at_opacity) + optional_shadow; tint_levels: tone1(5%_primary)+tone2(8%)+tone3(11%)+tone4(12%)+tone5(14%); effect: elevated_cards_appear_slightly_tinted_not_shadowed; tonalSurface: Card+Sheet+Dialog default; NO:adding_heavy_box_shadows_to_M3_components NO:using_M2_elevation_system_in_M3_app; eg: Card(elevation=8dp_shadow) >> Card(tonalElevation=4.dp) using_surface_tint

principle: Choose navigation component based on destination count — bottom bar for 3-5 destinations, navigation rail for tablet landscape, navigation drawer for 6+ destinations
explanation: X.mobile|m3_navigation: destination_count_determines_pattern; bottom_navigation_bar: 3-5_destinations+phone; navigation_rail: 3-7_destinations+tablet/landscape; navigation_drawer: 5+_destinations+tablet/desktop_or_collapsed_on_phone; modal_drawer: phone_with_many_destinations(behind_hamburger); NavigationBar_vs_DrawerSheet: NOT_mixed_on_same_screen; NO:hamburger_on_phone_with_3_destinations NO:bottom_bar_with_7_destinations; eg: 4_destinations_with_hamburger >> NavigationBar_with_4_items

principle: Use SF Symbols for all in-app icons — they inherit text weight, scale with Dynamic Type, adapt to dark mode, and support all 11 rendering modes automatically
explanation: X.mobile|sf_symbols: custom_icons: fixed_size+weight+color -> misaligned_with_system_font; SF_Symbols: vector+semantic_weight(ultralight_to_heavy)+multicolor+hierarchical+palette+monochrome; SwiftUI: Image(systemName:"star.fill")+.imageScale(.large); match_symbol_weight_to_adjacent_text_weight; NO:PNG_icons_next_to_SF_Symbol_text NO:custom_icons_for_concepts_covered_by_SF_Symbols; eg: custom_32px_star_PNG >> Image(systemName:"star.fill")+.fontWeight(.semibold)

principle: Apply Material Design 3 color roles not hardcoded colors — the role system (primary, on-primary, container, on-container) guarantees accessible contrast across all generated themes
explanation: X.mobile|m3_color_roles: hardcoded_hex -> breaks_when_theme_changes; M3_roles: primary(brand_CTA)+on-primary(text_on_primary)+primary-container(muted_bg)+on-primary-container(text_on_container)+secondary+tertiary+error+surface+on-surface; dynamic_color(Android12+): seed_color -> full_palette_generated_by_system; NO:hardcoded_colors; eg: Button(background=Color(0xFF6200EE)) >> Button(containerColor=MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary)

principle: Use Snackbar for brief non-blocking feedback and dialogs only for decisions requiring acknowledgment — most app feedback should not block the user's current flow
explanation: X.mobile|m3_feedback_patterns: dialog: use_only_for_irreversible_or_required_decisions; Snackbar: transient_feedback(3-4s)+optional_action(Undo); Toast_equivalent: Snackbar_without_action; banners: persistent_messages_requiring_action; inline_errors: for_form_validation(NO_dialogs_for_validation); NO:dialog_for_"operation_successful" NO:blocking_snackbar; eg: AlertDialog("Item_deleted","OK") >> Snackbar("Item_deleted",action="Undo")

principle: Use adaptive layouts with WindowSizeClass to serve phone, tablet, and foldable from one codebase — hardcoded phone layouts break on tablets and foldables
explanation: X.mobile|adaptive_layout: single_layout_hardcoded -> broken_on_tablet+foldable; WindowSizeClass: Compact(phone_portrait)+Medium(tablet_portrait/foldable)+Expanded(tablet_landscape); pattern: Compact=single_pane+BottomNav; NO:hardcoded_dp_widths_for_layout; eg: single_column_layout_hardcoded >> adaptive_WindowSizeClass_with_two-pane_on_Expanded

principle: Mobile touch targets must be at least 48x48dp (Android) or 44x44pt (iOS) — fingertips are imprecise and undersized targets cause errors that erode user trust faster than any other mobile mistake
explanation: X.mobile|touch_target: minimum: 48x48_dp_Android+44x44_pt_iOS; reason: finger_imprecise -> small_target -> missed_taps -> errors+frustration; applies_to: all_links+buttons+nav_items+form_controls; adjacent_targets_need_gap; most_mobile_errors_traced_to_target_too_small; NO:desktop_link_density_on_mobile NO:icon-only_buttons_smaller_than_44px; eg: small_text_links_on_touch_screen -> constant_wrong_taps >> large_tap_target_48dp -> accurate_every_time

principle: Use infinite scroll for bottomless lists like feeds and search results — appending content as the user scrolls avoids the context shift of paginated navigation and keeps desired items always reachable
