explanation: X.ui|visual_hierarchy: five_properties: clarity+actionability+affordance(looks_like_what_it_does)+consistency+alignment; affordance_example: slightly_3D_button -> signals_clickability; color_accessibility: 10%_men_colorblind -> reinforce_critical_distinctions_with_shape+text(never_color_alone); contrast: dark_on_light+light_on_dark; nothing_random_in_layout; NO:relying_on_color_alone_for_critical_distinctions; eg: OK_grayed_vs_enabled >> OK_with_checkmark+green_both -> accessible

principle: Use warm colors as small accents and cool colors as larger fields — warm colors advance and cool recede, so equal amounts of each create unbalanced visual weight
explanation: X.ui|warm_cool_colors: warm(red+yellow+orange): come_forward+jump_into_eyes -> use_less; cool(blue+green+purple): recede -> need_more_to_make_impact; balance_rule: dont_equal_amounts_hot+cool; NO:equal_areas_warm+cool; eg: red+blue_newsletter_equal_areas >> more_blue_ground+small_red_accent

principle: Show action buttons only on hover to avoid cluttering list interfaces with repeated controls — but always provide keyboard and context-menu alternatives since hover is invisible on touch screens
explanation: X.ui|hover_tools: use_when: list_items_with_2+_actions+showing_all_would_clutter; implementation: buttons_invisible_until_mouseover -> appear_on_hover; critical_caveat: hover_doesnt_exist_on_touch -> keyboard_shortcuts+context_menu_alternatives_required; NO:hover-only_for_the_only_way_to_perform_an_action_on_mobile; eg: email_list_cluttered_with_reply+archive_per_row >> hover_on_row -> actions_appear_cleanly

principle: A pattern library maintained by one discipline creates duplicates — designers who cannot find patterns create from scratch and developers tweak the result producing if-statements and exceptions
explanation: X.ui|multidisciplinary_library: single-discipline_library=fragile; dev-only: designers_don't_know_what_exists -> create_in_Photoshop -> devs_tweak_to_fit -> if_statements+duplicates; no_content_perspective: patterns_tied_to_specific_copy OR_content_forced_into_wrong_patterns; NO:library_built_and_maintained_by_one_discipline; eg: Sipgate_dev-only_library -> designers_bypassed_it -> devs_modified_designs -> duplicates_proliferate

principle: Make every metric on a dashboard directly actionable — a metric without a path to improve it is surveillance, not a tool
explanation: X.ui|actionable_metrics: metric_displayed_NO_action_path -> user_knows_number_but_cannot_respond; action_pattern: each_KPI_card_links_to_relevant_action_or_detail_view; examples: bounce_rate_card -> link_to_pages_report; NO:metrics_with_no_navigation_path; eg: churn_number_only >> churn_number+"View_at_risk_users"_link

principle: Never place neutral gray text on a colored background — use a color that shares the background hue at lower saturation to keep text visually connected to the surface
explanation: X.ui|colored_bg_text: pure_gray_on_colored_bg -> text_appears_detached+muddy; fix: text_color_shares_hue_of_bg + higher_lightness + lower_saturation; eg: hsl(220,15%,75%) on hsl(220,60%,30%) == harmonious; white_text: valid_on_dark_surfaces; test: does_text_feel_like_it_belongs_to_this_surface? NO:#888_on_blue_card NO:opacity_hack_on_white; eg: gray-500_text_on_blue_card >> hsl(220,20%,70%)_text_on_blue_card

principle: Position the single most important metric in the top-left card of every dashboard — eye tracking on information-dense screens shows users fixate top-left first and use it to calibrate the scale of all other numbers
explanation: X.ui|dashboard_primary_metric: random_metric_placement -> user_hunts_for_the_number_they_care_about; top_left_rule: most_business_critical_KPI(revenue+DAU+churn) always_top_left_card; supporting_metrics: clockwise_from_primary; NO:burying_primary_KPI_in_middle; eg: 6_equal_cards >> primary_KPI_top_left_2x_size+supporting_metrics_smaller

principle: Group inventory elements by behavior they enable not by visual appearance — two elements that look different but do the same thing belong in the same group and are consolidation candidates
explanation: X.ui|purpose_directed_inventory: visual_inventory: groups_by_type(buttons/tabs/menus)->misses_same_action_done_differently; purpose_directed: group_by_behavior -> different-looking_elements_in_same_group_if_same_purpose; reveals_consolidation_candidates; NO:visual-type_grouping_as_primary_step; eg: Refine_list_behavior -> tabs_on_catalog+dropdown_on_discovery -> same_group -> one_canonical_pattern_identified

principle: Expand any color relationship by using tints and shades — adding black creates shades and adding white creates tints, dramatically increasing palette options while preserving harmony
explanation: X.ui|tints_and_shades: pure_hues_only->limited+too_vibrant; shade:hue+black; tint:hue+white; tone:hue+gray; 1_hue->9_usable_variants(100->900_lightness_scale); brand_palette: 1_pure_hue+4_tints+4_shades->rich_system; NO:only_pure_hues; eg: single_blue_brand_color >> blue_50+100+200+400+600+800_scale->backgrounds+text+borders_all_harmonious

principle: Squint your eyes and count where they stop — if more than three to five visual stopping points exist, group elements into fewer visual units
explanation: X.ui|squint_test: squint_eyes -> count_where_eye_stops == count_visual_elements; if_count > 5 -> find_elements_that_belong_together -> group_into_proximity; side_effect: white_space_becomes_organized_automatically; purpose: fewer_visual_units -> clearer_organization+easier_reading_path; NO:counting_individual_text_items NO:grouping_unrelated_things_just_to_reduce_count; eg: resume_12_equal-spaced_sections >> 4_grouped_units(header+experience+education+contact)

principle: Manually kern display type at large sizes — automatic kerning is insufficient above display size because optical inconsistency between letter pairs becomes clearly visible and looks unprofessional
explanation: X.ui|manual_kerning: large_type: awkward_letterspacing -> naive+unprofessional; kerning: removing_tiny_space_between_specific_character_pairs(WA_LO_AT_To); goal: make_space_appear_VISUALLY_consistent NOT_physically_equal; NO:relying_on_auto_kern_for_headlines; eg: WATERMELON_no_kern -> awkward_W-A_gap >> WATERMELON_hand_kerned -> visually_even

principle: Design the empty state of every component before designing the full version — empty states are the first-run experience and components designed only with data look broken when empty
explanation: X.ui|empty_state_first: design_with_data -> empty_state_afterthought -> onboarding_broken; empty_state_formula: (1)illustration_or_icon_for_context (2)why_it_is_empty (3)primary_CTA_to_fill_it; required_states: 0_items+1_item+many_items+loading; NO:blank_white_area_with_no_guidance NO:designing_only_with_perfect_dummy_data; eg: empty_list_shows_nothing >> icon_+_"Add_your_first_project"_+_CTA_button

principle: Use sparklines for trend context inside metric cards and full charts only when the user needs to interact with the data — full charts in a summary dashboard consume space without adding actionable detail
explanation: X.ui|sparklines_vs_full_charts: full_chart_per_KPI_card -> dashboard_too_dense+overwhelming; sparkline: 40-60px_tall_line_chart_embedded_in_card -> shows_direction+volatility without_needing_axes_or_labels; use_full_chart: dedicated_analytics_page+when_user_needs_drill_down+hover_tooltips; NO:mini_pie_charts_in_cards(unreadable_at_small_size); eg: full_30-day_chart_in_KPI_card >> sparkline_embedded_in_metric_card

principle: Place a short example or explanation beside every text field whose expected format is not obvious — visually separate it from the label so expert users can ignore hints without breaking their scanning rhythm
explanation: X.ui|input_hints: what: phrase_or_example_beside_or_below_text_field; why: frees_user_from_guessing_format+context_label_alone_cant_provide; placement: below_or_beside+visually_distinct_from_label; NO:no_hint_on_ambiguous_fields(phone+date+username+password_rules); eg: Date_field_no_hint -> format_errors >> Date_field+(MM/DD/YYYY_hint) -> zero_format_errors

principle: Place form error messages inline next to the originating field — modal error dialogs and separate error screens both force users to dismiss or navigate before fixing the problem they can no longer see
explanation: X.ui|error_messages: placement: inline_on_form+next_to_originating_field; content: which_field+why_wrong+how_to_fix; avoid: modal_error_dialog(dismiss_to_fix -> error_message_gone) + error_screen_after_submit(Back_button -> errors_gone); NO:modal_dialog_for_form_errors; eg: submit -> separate_error_page -> click_Back -> errors_gone >> inline_red_field_with_fix_hint -> fix_without_losing_context

principle: Group related items into close proximity to create one visual unit — scattered equal-spaced elements imply no relationships and force the reader to figure out the organization themselves
explanation: X.ui|proximity_grouping: equal_spacing_between_all -> no_implied_relationships -> reader_must_figure_out_hierarchy; group_related: items_that_belong_together -> close_proximity; unrelated_items -> physically_separated; NO:hit_Return_between_every_element; eg: business_card_5_scattered_items >> name_group+contact_group_with_clear_separation

principle: Provide immediate visual feedback on every interactive element within 100ms of interaction — any delay longer than 100ms creates uncertainty about whether the tap or click registered
explanation: X.ui|immediate_feedback: 100ms_threshold: human_perception_boundary_for_causality; hover:change_within_16ms(1_frame); click/tap:visual_change_within_100ms; if_real_action_takes_longer: show_loading_state_immediately+do_work_in_background; optimistic_UI: update_state_before_server_confirms; NO:button_that_does_nothing_visually_on_click NO:100ms_delay_before_hover_state; eg: button_click_with_300ms_server_round_trip_before_any_change >> immediate_spinner_on_click+server_update_on_complete

principle: Build a 9-shade color scale per hue before designing any component — picking shades on the fly produces multiple slightly-different versions of the same color fighting each other
explanation: X.ui|palette_first: ad_hoc_shade_picking -> 7_competing_blues_in_one_UI; correct: define_before_design: 50(bg)+100+200+300+400+500(default)+600(hover)+700(active)+800+900(text); Tailwind_palette == validated_reference; mid-design_additions -> only_within_defined_scale; NO:hex_picked_per_component NO:fewer_than_5_shades_for_any_primary_color; eg: random_shades_per_card >> single_9-shade_scale_applied_consistently

principle: Invest in signature moments — small interactions with meaning become product differentiators; TED's play button ripple echoes their video drop animation; a single signature moment feels disconnected until it is echoed elsewhere throughout the interface
explanation: X.ui|signature_moments: small_interactions=differentiators_when_they_have_meaning+story; TED_ripple<-inspired_by_drop_animation_in_video_intros; FutureLearn_circular_bouncy_tick->felt_disconnected_until_echoed_in_other_progress_indicators; design_system_must_preserve_space_to_nurture_these; NO:treating_microinteractions_as_optional_polish; eg: plain_checkbox >> circular_bouncy_tick_animation -> learner_feedback_cites_feeling_of_accomplishment

principle: Group dashboard widgets into information layers: summary at top, detail in middle, raw data at bottom — users scan top-to-bottom from overview to specifics, and raw data shown first creates cognitive overload
explanation: X.ui|dashboard_information_layers: raw_data_at_top: user_must_process_details_before_context; information_hierarchy: (1)summary_KPIs: key_numbers_at_a_glance (2)trend_charts: patterns_over_time (3)segment_breakdowns: which_group_is_driving_the_number (4)raw_tables: drill_down_for_individual_records; visual_grouping: each_layer_has_section_header+consistent_spacing; NO:tables_at_top_of_dashboard; eg: raw_event_table_first >> KPI_summary+charts+segments+table

principle: Encode critical data distinctions using preattentive visual variables — color, size, shape, and orientation are processed in parallel before conscious attention, so key data points pop out regardless of how many items surround them
explanation: X.ui|preattentive_variables: preattentive_variables: color+size+shape+orientation+position+motion+enclosure+line_weight; key_property: processed_parallel(constant_find_time) NOT linear_scan(read_every_value); use_multiple_variables_for_multidimensional_data; NO:all_data_points_same_visual_weight(forces_scanning_every_value); eg: 100_numbers_find_values>1 -> read_each_one >> large_font_for_outliers -> found_immediately

principle: Build UI systems bottom-up from atoms to molecules to organisms — a style change at the atomic level propagates automatically to every component that inherits it, making global updates instantaneous
explanation: X.ui|atomic_design: hierarchy: atoms(text_field+color+typeface) -> molecules(input+label+hint+button) -> organisms(full_header_with_nav+search+avatar) -> templates -> pages; key_property: styling_inherits_upward -> change_color_token_once -> updates_everywhere; style_guide_first: color+typography+grid+icons; NO:building_screens_without_component_system; eg: change_primary_button_color_in_one_token >> propagates_to_all_organisms_automatically

principle: Choose system strictness by which downside you can manage not by team size — strict systems create UX compromises for consistency; loose systems degrade without shared knowledge ingrained in culture
explanation: X.ui|strictness_choice: strict=predictable+consistent BUT_rigid->UX_sacrificed_for_consistency; loose=experimental+context_sensitive BUT_messy_without_shared_culture; NO:assuming_larger_team->stricter_needed; rule: right_system=one_where_team_manages_its_downsides; eg: Airbnb(strict+centralized)->predictable_at_scale; TED(loose+distributed)->brand_acumen_over_rules -> both_effective_for_different_reasons

principle: Color system consistency means consistent meaning not consistent values — twenty shades of blue are fine if blue always means interactive; the problem is blue headings users cannot click when blue is also used for links
explanation: X.ui|color_meaning_over_values: hex_palette_alone!=consistent_use; shared_meaning_in_context_required; 20_shades_of_blue=not_a_problem_if_consistently_means_interactive; problem=blue_headings+blue_links_on_same_page(usability_confusion); reserve_emotionally_loaded_color_for_its_moment; NO:defining_color_system_as_palette_only; eg: FutureLearn_full_gradient=celebration_only -> reuse_in_promo_weakens_celebratory_association

principle: Define brand personality with 5 to 7 traits plus explicit traits to avoid — MailChimp's 'Fun but not childish, powerful but not complicated' prevents more design mistakes than a list of positive traits alone ever could
explanation: X.ui|brand_persona_traits: 5-7_traits+explicit_opposing_limits; paired_format(positive+negative): prevents_overreach_into_adjacent_wrong_territory; MailChimp: Fun_but_not_childish+Funny_but_not_goofy+Powerful_but_not_complicated+Hip_but_not_alienating+Informal_but_not_sloppy; NO:traits_without_explicit_opposing_limits; eg: "friendly" >> "friendly_but_not_unprofessional" -> distinct_visual_boundaries

principle: A dashboard's power comes from editorial restraint — remove every data point that doesn't help the user take action, and keep everything visible on one screen without scrolling
explanation: X.ui|dashboard: purpose: quick_status+key_metrics+next_actions; editorial_rule: eliminate_confusing_data+deemphasize_unimportant -> user_picks_out_what_matters; layout: visual_hierarchy+grouped_Titled_Sections+one_page_preferred; failure: too_much_data -> dashboard_becomes_noise_user_ignores; NO:dumping_all_metrics_on_one_screen NO:unactionable_vanity_metrics_as_primary_content; eg: 20_charts_one_page >> 5_critical_KPIs_with_trend_arrows+one_CTA -> user_acts_immediately

principle: Never set extended body text in all caps — lowercase words are recognized by their overall shape but all-cap words are uniformly rectangular and must be read letter by letter, dramatically slowing reading
explanation: X.ui|avoid_all_caps: lowercase_readability: recognize_words_by_shape+coastline; all_caps: every_word == same_rectangular_form -> letter_by_letter_reading -> slower+tiring; acceptable_all_caps: short_word+logo_acronym+deliberate_design_statement+when_lower_readability_consciously_justified; NO:all_caps_for_emphasis_in_body; eg: menu_ALL_CAPS >> caps+lowercase -> more_readable+takes_less_space

principle: Draw a module's content structure before designing it visually — agree on required elements optional elements and hierarchy with the full team; this aligns design and development intent before any visual decisions commit anyone
explanation: X.ui|content_structure_first: before_visual_design: list_required_elements+decide_hierarchy+grouping+sketch_structure; eg_Billboard: heading(required)+strong_CTA(required)+background(required)+label(optional); NOT_a_wireframe -> focused_on_content_hierarchy_specifically; designer+developer+content_work_in_parallel_once_structure_agreed; NO:starting_visual_comp_before_content_agreement; eg: promo_module_debate -> 5min_structure_sketch -> 2hrs_parallel_design+dev_work

principle: Never combine two script typefaces or a script with an italic — both share flowing slanted form creating conflict; pair scripts with strong roman faces instead
explanation: X.ui|no_script_italic_combo: scripts+italics: share_similar_form(cursive+flowing+slanted); two_scripts_together: too_many_similarities -> conflict; script+italic: same_form_type -> conflict; NO:two_calligraphic_scripts_on_same_piece; eg: Peoni_script+Goudy_Oldstyle_Italic >> conflict >> Peoni_script+Aachen_Bold_roman >> clear_contrast

principle: Strong alignment — not typeface choice alone — creates a sophisticated look; it is the invisible factor that most separates a polished design from an amateurish one
explanation: X.ui|alignment_sophistication: common_mistake: changing_font_but_keeping_weak_alignment -> still_looks_amateurish; diagnosis: piece_looks_dull? -> check_alignment_before_changing_typefaces; strong_flush_alignment: communicates_organization+confidence+intentionality; NO:changing_font_as_first_fix; eg: business_card_3_alignments+nice_font >> one_alignment+simple_font -> dramatically_better

principle: Show a loading indicator the moment a user submits an action — users with visible feedback wait measurably longer without frustration than those watching a frozen interface
explanation: X.ui|spinners: trigger: user_submits_action -> show_immediately_before_response; spinner=stateless_animation(unknown_duration); loading_indicator=progress_meter+percentage(known_duration); NO:UI_frozen_with_no_feedback_during_operation; eg: form_submit_freezes_5s_silently -> user_clicks_again >> spinner_appears_immediately -> user_waits_patiently

principle: Use skeleton screens for content loading over 300ms and no loading indicator for content loading under 300ms — instant transitions feel broken, skeletons prevent layout shift
explanation: X.ui|loading_state_timing: <300ms: no_indicator_needed(flash_of_loading_state_feels_worse); 300ms-1s: skeleton_screen(same_layout_as_content); >1s: skeleton+progress_or_contextual_message; skeleton_rules: match_layout_exactly + animate_shimmer(1.5s_left-to-right_gradient); NO:spinner_for_every_async_operation NO:skeleton_that_differs_from_final_layout; NO:no_feedback_for_slow_operations; eg: spinner_for_100ms_fetch >> nothing(fast) OR skeleton(slow)

principle: Invest in a typeface family with multiple weights rather than multiple families — light through ultra-black within one family enables dramatic contrast while guaranteeing harmony
explanation: X.ui|type_family_contrast: combining_different_typefaces: risk_of_conflict+requires_skill; single_family_multiple_weights: Avenir_Next_Condensed_UltraLight+Heavy+Italic -> dramatic_contrast+guaranteed_harmony; benefit: all_members_share_underlying_structure -> no_conflict_possible; NO:only_owning_Helvetica_and_Arial; eg: Helvetica_Regular+Helvetica_Bold_weak >> Avenir_Condensed_UltraLight+Avenir_Heavy_dramatic

principle: A typographic scale standardizes sizes but not usage — add context guidelines specifying which size applies to which hierarchy level or designers will still pick inconsistently
explanation: X.ui|typography_system_vs_scale: scale=standardizes_sizes_only; system=scale+usage_guidelines(which_size_for_which_context+hierarchy_rules); standardized_scale_alone: headings_still_inconsistent_because_designers_unsure_which_to_pick; NO:treating_type_scale_as_complete_typography_system; eg: FutureLearn_unified_all_text_sizes -> headings_still_varied -> added_context_guidelines+hierarchy_rules -> consistent_use_achieved

principle: Match the data visualization model to the data structure — the same dataset shown as a table versus a map reveals completely different patterns, and the wrong model actively hides insight
explanation: X.ui|dataviz_model: models: linear(list)+tabular(spreadsheet)+hierarchical(tree)+network(flowchart)+geographic(map/scatter)+textual(word_cloud); selection_rule: if_data_fits_multiple_models -> consider_which_plays_up_which_aspects; wrong_model: geographic_data_as_table -> geographic_patterns_invisible; NO:always_defaulting_to_table_for_any_data; eg: sales_by_region_as_spreadsheet >> sales_by_region_as_map -> cluster_patterns_visible_instantly

principle: Support multiple simultaneous workspaces so users can compare and keep intentions visible — forcing single-document mode is the main source of unnecessary context-switching friction
explanation: X.ui|many_workspaces: need: parallel_work+comparison+context_switching_NO_losing_state; implementations: browser_tabs+OS_windows+split_panels+tab_groups; supports: Prospective_Memory(open_tab_as_reminder)+Safe_Exploration(new_tab_without_losing_original); NO:forcing_single_document_mode_for_comparison_tasks; eg: code+preview_side_by_side >> fullscreen_editor_only -> constant_alt-tab_friction

principle: Define a visual framework once and apply it to every screen — colors, fonts, spacing, and writing style specified in a single system mean any new screen automatically looks on-brand
explanation: X.ui|visual_framework: all_screen_templates_share: layout_grid+background+accent_colors+font_scale+writing_style+spacing; implementation: CSS_design_tokens+stylesheet -> change_once_applies_everywhere; benefit: separates_style_from_content -> tweak_independently; NO:each_screen_designed_independently; eg: TED_site_limited_color+layout_grid -> any_new_page_immediately_looks_on_brand >> mismatched_per-page_styles -> inconsistent_experience

principle: Keep micro-interaction duration between 100ms and 500ms — below 100ms is imperceptible, above 500ms feels like the UI is responding slowly
explanation: X.ui|animation_duration: <100ms == imperceptible; 100-300ms == snappy_micro(hover+toggle+ripple); 200-400ms == standard_UI_transition(drawer+sheet+modal); 300-500ms == complex_layout_change; >500ms == perceived_as_loading_not_animation; distance_rule: longer_travel -> slightly_longer_duration; test: if_it_feels_slow -> cut_50ms; NO:500ms_button_hover NO:100ms_full_page_transition; eg: drawer_open_800ms >> drawer_open_280ms_ease-out

principle: Use only one text alignment per page — mixing centered, flush left, and flush right on the same piece creates visual chaos even when each individual element looks acceptable on its own
explanation: X.ui|one_alignment_rule: multiple_alignments: centered+flush_left+flush_right_on_same_card -> chaotic+amateurish; single_alignment: everything_flush_left -> all_pieces_share_same_invisible_edge -> unified; clarification: one_alignment_doesnt_mean_same_x_position -> means_same_alignment_TYPE; NO:three_alignment_styles_on_business_card; eg: card_centered+flush_left+flush_right >> everything_flush_right_creating_one_clean_edge

principle: Start every layout with more whitespace than you think you need and remove it selectively — cramped layouts result from building into already-tight containers
explanation: X.ui|whitespace_first: assemble_in_tight_container -> squeeze_to_fit -> ugly_forced; correct: generous_padding(section:64-80px, card:32px, inline:16-24px) -> add_elements -> tighten_selectively; rule: if_it_feels_like_too_much -> probably_right; whitespace_signal: premium; NO:8px_padding_everywhere NO:tighten_first_then_breathe; eg: card_padding_8px_cramped >> card_padding_32px_then_reduce_where_justified

principle: Layer two box-shadows to simulate realistic depth — a single shadow reads as CSS, two shadows (one tight directional and one large ambient) read as light
explanation: X.ui|two_layer_shadow: single_shadow == artificial_feel; two_layers: (1)directional: 0_1px_3px_rgba(0,0,0,0.06) + (2)ambient: 0_8px_24px_rgba(0,0,0,0.04) -> light_simulation; shadow_color: never_pure_black -> tint_with_surface_hue; elevation_scale: 3_sizes(card/modal/dropdown) each with own two-shadow pair; NO:box-shadow_single_dark_heavy NO:identical_shadow_on_all_elements; eg: 0_4px_6px_rgba(0,0,0,0.5) >> 0_1px_2px_rgba(0,20,60,0.05)+0_8px_20px_rgba(0,20,60,0.04)

principle: Modular systems produce generic designs — to preserve distinctiveness compensate with strong content voice and perceptual patterns and design the connections between modules
explanation: X.ui|modularity_tradeoff: modules_must_be_generic_to_accommodate_variety -> predictable_designs+composition_story_lost; fix: distinct_content+strong_voice+effective_perceptual_patterns; focus_on_connections_between_modules(visual_loudness+hierarchy+role_in_journey) NOT_only_modules; NO:forcing_reuse_at_cost_of_page_impact; eg: FutureLearn_over-reused_modules -> added_custom_branded_sections_for_high-impact_pages

principle: Design in grayscale before adding color — if hierarchy is not clear in black and white, color will decorate the problem not solve it
explanation: X.ui|grayscale_first: color_layered_on_broken_hierarchy -> problem_persists; correct_order: size+weight+spacing -> grayscale_hierarchy_solid -> add_color_for_personality+meaning; test: desaturate_design -> primary_CTA_must_still_dominate_all_competitors; NO:relying_on_color_as_only_hierarchy_signal NO:adding_color_before_layout_validated; eg: blue_button_hierarchy >> same_size_weight_white_button_failing >> fix_size+weight THEN add_color

principle: Use paragraph space-before and space-after settings — not the Return key — to control exact spacing between elements and achieve precise visual groupings
explanation: X.ui|paragraph_spacing_control: using_Return_key -> only_full_line_increments -> no_precise_control; style_sheets: set_exact_space_before+after_each_element_type; benefit: tight_leading_within_group + more_space_between_groups; NO:double_Return_between_every_item; eg: menu_dish_name_close_to_description_but_more_space_before_next_dish

principle: Design hover states as a preview of the click state at reduced intensity — hover at 8% opacity overlay, active/pressed at 16% overlay, creates a clear three-state system
explanation: X.ui|hover_active_states: hover: shows_interactivity(cursor:pointer+visual_change); active: confirms_press(darker_than_hover); three_state: default+hover(8%_overlay)+active(16%_overlay); implementation: CSS::hover{filter:brightness(0.95)} ::active{filter:brightness(0.9)} OR opacity_overlay; focus-visible: keyboard_focus_ring_must_be_visible(WCAG_2.4.7); NO:hover_state_identical_to_default NO:missing_active_state; eg: button_no_hover_state >> button_hover:bg_10%_darker+active:bg_20%_darker

principle: Use cards for lists where every item shares the same structure and behaviors — design the max-content and min-content variants first to ensure the layout works at both extremes before filling in the rest
explanation: X.ui|cards: use_when: items_share_same_behaviors(image+title+description+action)+need_responsive_reflow; design_process: mock_up_max-content_item+min-content_item -> tweak_until_both_readable -> fill_rest; decide: icon_actions(save_space) vs text_actions(clarity); NO:cards_when_items_have_completely_different_structures; eg: Pinterest+Airbnb+news_articles -> perfect_card_candidates >> mixed_structure_items -> use_table_or_list

principle: Use a carousel for horizontally browsable visual items when vertical space is scarce — it encourages serendipitous discovery but prevents jumping deep into the list, so avoid it for task-critical navigation
explanation: X.ui|carousel: use_when: visual_items_uniquely_identified_by_image+similar_size+flat_list+limited_vertical_space; benefit: compact_vertical_footprint+engaging_horizontal_browsing; limitation: user_cannot_jump_to_deep_position -> must_scroll_through_everything -> encourages_serendipity; NO:carousel_for_task-critical_navigation; eg: Apple_TV_row+Netflix_genre_rows -> compact+browsable >> full_grid -> vertical_space_consumed

principle: Create visual hierarchy by de-emphasizing secondary content rather than inflating primary content — making less important elements lighter achieves focus without size wars
explanation: X.ui|deemphasis_hierarchy: primary_emphasis_trap -> inflate_font_size -> cramped_competing_elements; de-emphasis: secondary_color: gray-400_vs_gray-900 + weight: regular_vs_semibold -> primary_reads_first; test: cover_primary -> secondary_should_visually_recede; NO:making_everything_bold_to_compete NO:same_text_color_for_all_content; eg: all_card_items_same_weight >> title_gray-900_semibold+meta_gray-400_regular+timestamp_gray-300_small

principle: Divide dense pages into named sections with strong visual titles — if you cannot give a section a short memorable name, the grouping itself is wrong and needs restructuring
explanation: X.ui|titled_sections: purpose: structure_dense_content_into_scannable_chunks; benefit: IA_obvious_at_glance+eye_guided_through_page; diagnostic: cannot_name_section_briefly -> grouping_is_wrong(not_a_naming_problem); NO:long_unsectioned_page_of_mixed_content; eg: settings_page_one_long_scroll >> settings_grouped_Security/Notifications/Account -> instantly_scannable

principle: Use contrast to serve both organization and visual interest simultaneously — a reader should glance at a page and instantly understand its hierarchy before reading a single word
explanation: X.ui|contrast_dual_purpose: two_purposes: (1)create_visual_interest -> page_more_likely_to_be_read (2)organize_information -> reader_understands_flow_instantly; inseparable: organized_page == visually_interesting; contrasting_elements: should_NEVER_confuse_reader; NO:decorative_contrast_that_confuses_hierarchy; eg: resume_uniform_type >> bold_section_headers+contrasting_job_titles -> skimmable_hierarchy

principle: Identify your existing consistencies and amplify them into deliberate graphic statements — upgrading a 14pt bold headline to 16pt ultra-bold turns passive consistency into active design
explanation: X.ui|amplify_consistency: existing_consistency: headlines_all_same_size == weak_passive_repetition; amplify: take_consistent_element -> push_stronger+more_dramatic; benefit: not_only_interesting_page_but_increased_clarity; NO:starting_with_new_elements_before_strengthening_existing; eg: 14pt_Times_Bold_headlines >> 16pt_Mikado_Ultra_headlines -> more_visual_interest+stronger_organization

principle: If a designer thinks a visual cue sticks out like a sore thumb it probably needs to be twice as prominent — users scan at speed designers never match
explanation: X.ui|visual_cue_doubling: designer_taste = subtle(sophistication_bias); user_reality = hurry+scan+miss_subtle; rule: when_tuning_prominence_of_active_state+you_are_here+error_highlight+primary_CTA -> if_it_feels_obvious_to_you -> double_it; NO:relying_on_single_subtle_cue(thin_bottom-border_for_active_tab); eg: active_nav_item=slightly_darker_grey_text >> active_nav_item=bold + accent_color + left_border_stripe

principle: Use module tabs when users need only one content section at a time and sections are of similar height — make selected state visually unambiguous through more than color alone
explanation: X.ui|module_tabs: use_when: heterogeneous_content+no_room_for_all+one_section_at_a_time+similar_height+fewer_than_10+static_set; selection_indicator: tab_contiguous_with_panel(not_color_alone); tabs_can_be: left_column+below+turned_sideways; vs_accordion: tabs=mutually_exclusive >> accordion=user_can_open_multiple_simultaneously; NO:color_only_to_show_selection NO:too_many_tabs_to_fit_in_one_row; eg: product_Description/Reviews/Specs_tabs -> one_shown_at_a_time

principle: Set each form input width to match the expected length of its value — an oversized input for a short fixed-length value communicates designer misunderstanding and undermines form credibility
explanation: X.ui|input_width_affordance: input_width == implicit_signal_of_expected_value_length; full_width: name+email+address(variable); fixed_narrow: phone+zip+CVV+date(known_length); implementation: width_in_ch_units OR fixed_px; principle: user_should_not_wonder_how_much_to_type; NO:full_width_inputs_for_all_fields NO:tiny_inputs_for_long_free_text; eg: 100%_width_input_for_4-digit_PIN >> 64px_fixed_width_for_PIN+full_width_for_address

principle: Find the most important element and make it the clear focal point with strong contrast — readers who see a clear focal point will follow the visual hierarchy you create from there
explanation: X.ui|contrast_focal_point: no_focal_point: reader_doesnt_know_where_to_start -> everything_equal_weight -> nothing_read; create_focal_point: most_interesting_OR_most_important_element -> emphasize_strongly_with_contrast; visual_hierarchy: strong_focal_point -> reader_enters -> follows_path; NO:making_everything_the_same_size; eg: all-caps_centered_pie_shop_ad >> large_pie_image+strong_shop_name+contrasting_list

principle: Use sequential color palettes for ordered data and qualitative palettes for categorical data — using a rainbow palette for ordered data makes the viewer perceive false categorical groupings
explanation: X.ui|data_viz_color: rainbow_palette_for_temperature_map -> no_perceptual_order; correct_palettes: sequential(single_hue_light-to-dark) for ordered_data(low_to_high); diverging(two_hues_meeting_at_neutral) for_data_with_meaningful_midpoint(positive_vs_negative); NO:rainbow_for_continuous_data; eg: rainbow_heatmap >> single_hue_sequential_palette

principle: Never use the underline button for emphasis — underlines were typewriter instructions to typesetters and signal amateur work; use bold, italic, size change, or color instead
explanation: X.ui|no_underlines: underline_origin: typewriter_instruction_to_typesetter -> "make_this_italic"; today: you_ARE_the_typesetter -> set_italic_directly; emphasis_alternatives: bold+larger_size+different_font+color+set_apart_spatially; if_you_must_have_line: customize_thickness+distance+length -> do_not_use_default_underline_button; NO:underline_button_for_emphasis NO:default_underline_cutting_through_descenders; eg: "Important Notice" underlined >> "Important Notice" in bold_italic

principle: Connect every element to something else on the page with a visual alignment — an element placed arbitrarily with no alignment to any other item makes the whole design look unorganized
explanation: X.ui|alignment_connection: nothing_arbitrary: every_element_must_connect_visually_to_another; invisible_line: aligned_elements_share_invisible_connecting_line_visible_to_eye; even_when_separated: elements_far_apart_appear_unified_when_sharing_alignment; test: draw_lines_between_elements -> where_are_connections_missing?; NO:throwing_elements_on_page_to_fill_space NO:each_element_floating_independently; eg: business_card_3_different_alignments >> all_items_flush_right_creating_single_strong_edge

principle: Only break alignment boldly and completely — a timid half-break looks like a mistake, but a dramatic deliberate break against a strong alignment background reads as intentional
explanation: X.ui|intentional_alignment_break: timid_break: slightly_off_alignment -> looks_like_mistake; bold_break: dramatically_off -> looks_intentional; prerequisite: strong_consistent_alignment_established_first -> reader_knows_the_rule -> break_registers_as_deliberate; rule: must_know_rule_before_breaking_it; NO:breaking_alignment_before_establishing_strong_alignment NO:being_a_wimp; eg: illustration_angled_breaking_into_text >> works_because_rest_of_piece_has_clean_alignments

principle: Set extended body copy in oldstyle serif typefaces — their moderate thick/thin transitions are invisible at reading size and let readers absorb content without the type calling attention to itself
explanation: X.ui|oldstyle_for_body: oldstyle_features: diagonal_stress+bracketed_serifs+moderate_thick-thin -> based_on_pen_handlettering; readability: no_distinguishing_characteristics -> doesnt_interrupt_reading -> best_for_extensive_text; avoid_for_body: modern_typefaces(Bodoni+Didot): radical_thick-thin+vertical_stress -> dazzling_at_small_sizes+thin_lines_disappear; NO:Bodoni_for_body_copy; eg: Bodoni_body_copy -> dazzling+hard_to_read >> Garamond_body -> invisible+readable

principle: Use typographer's curly quotes and true apostrophes — straight typewriter marks are an instant signal of amateur work that professionals notice immediately even in million-dollar ads
explanation: X.ui|typographers_quotes: straight_quotes -> typewriter_marks+amateurish; true_quotes: opening=shaped_like_6s, closing=shaped_like_9s; apostrophe: single_closing_quote(shape_of_9); NO:straight_apostrophe_in_any_published_work; eg: "Save yourself," she said >> “Save yourself,” she said (proper_curled_marks)

principle: Use color wheel relationships to guarantee working palettes — complementary (opposites), triads (equidistant thirds), split complements, and analogous (neighbors) all produce harmonious combinations
explanation: X.ui|color_wheel_relationships: random_picking->unpredictable; relationships: complementary(180°=high_contrast)+triadic(120°=balanced)+analogous(adjacent=harmonious)+split_complement(safer_than_pure_comp); pick_primary->apply_rule->guaranteed_harmony; NO:random_color_selection; eg: need_accent_for_blue_brand >> opposite_on_wheel=orange->guaranteed_contrast+harmony

principle: Set key phrases in bold within gray body text to create entry points — a completely gray page discourages casual readers, but bold phrases let eyes scan and pull readers into the full text
explanation: X.ui|bold_in_body: gray_page: no_contrast -> discourages_casual_reader; bold_phrases: create_visual_entry_points -> reader_scans_bold_first -> pulled_into_full_text; mechanism: lighter_commitment_to_scan -> reader_reads_bold -> bound_to_read_more; NO:gray_wall_of_text_no_entry_points; eg: medical_flyer_all_same_size >> bold_key_symptoms+bold_benefits -> entry_points

principle: Place the primary content in the largest area with secondary tools clustered around it — users assess peripheral elements in relation to what's center, which eliminates the scanning effort of figuring out what comes first
explanation: X.ui|center_stage: layout_rule: primary_content_2x_wider_than_side_margins+2x_taller_than_top_bottom; eye_flow: user_goes_to_center_first -> then_assesses_periphery_relative_to_center; use_when: single_unit_of_content+document_editing+primary_task+article_pages+maps; NO:equal-weight_two-column_layout_for_primary_task_screen; eg: two_equal_columns -> user_unsure_where_to_start >> large_canvas+small_side_tools -> primary_task_is_obvious

principle: Name interface patterns with verbs that describe user actions not nouns that describe appearance — 'Course banner' limits the pattern to one content type; 'Billboard' which promotes anything unlocks reuse across courses events and articles
explanation: X.ui|pattern_naming_verbs: noun_name(Course_banner/Image_header)->tied_to_presentation->limits_use_cases; verb_name(Billboard/Promote)->describes_action->broader_applicability; process: ask_what_does_this_pattern_DO? -> name_from_behavior; NO:names_from_visual_appearance_or_content_type; eg: "Course_banner" >> "Billboard" -> same_module_reused_for_events+discussions+promotions+articles_without_creating_new_variants

principle: Proximity communicates relationship — elements close together imply connection, so never place unrelated items near each other just to fill space
explanation: X.ui|proximity_implies_relationship: elements_close -> reader_assumes_relationship; filling_space_by_pushing_things_together -> creates_false_relationships; check: is_bullet_closer_to_its_text_or_to_next_item?; test: remove_element_and_see_if_remaining_group_still_makes_sense; NO:sticking_items_in_corners_because_space_is_empty NO:bullet_closer_to_previous_item_than_to_its_own_text; eg: newsletter_banner_random_placement >> vol_number+date_together_separate_from_tagline

principle: Start color selection from a logical anchor — season, brand colors, a photograph in the piece, or target emotion — then build the palette from that starting point
explanation: X.ui|color_selection_strategy: overwhelming_to_choose -> use_logical_anchor; strategies: (1)season: summer=warm_reds+yellows, winter=cool_blues (2)brand_colors: use_tints+shades_of_existing (3)photograph: eyedropper_from_image -> build_analogous_or_complementary (4)emotional_target: calm=analogous, exciting=complementary; NO:random_favorite_colors; eg: random_colors_for_seasonal_brochure >> pick_autumn_analogous_colors_from_photo

principle: Classify interactive elements as CTAs or links by interaction type not by markup — a CTA can be marked up as a link or a button; primary CTA appears exactly once per screen
explanation: X.ui|cta_classification: CTA=prominent_standalone_action; link=embedded_optional_pathway; CTA_markup_depends_on_interaction: action_on_current_page=button; navigate_away=CTA_link; primary_CTA=only_one_per_screen(most_important_action_read_out_first); NO:classifying_button_vs_link_by_markup_alone; eg: View_book_expands_inline->CTA_button; same_opens_new_page->CTA_link -> accessible+consistent+simple_code

principle: Select chart type by the relationship being shown: bar for comparison, line for trend over time, scatter for correlation, pie only for two-part wholes
explanation: X.ui|chart_type_selection: wrong_chart_type -> data_relationship_misrepresented; rules: bar: compare_discrete_categories(A_vs_B_vs_C); line: continuous_change_over_time(daily_revenue); scatter: relationship_between_two_variables; pie: ONLY_two_or_three_slices_that_sum_to_whole(market_share); heat_map: patterns_across_two_dimensions; NO:pie_chart_with_7+_slices(impossible_to_compare) NO:bar_chart_for_continuous_time_series; eg: 8-slice_pie_chart >> horizontal_bar_chart_with_sorted_values

principle: Separate pattern library from design system — a library captures patterns but the system is the connected patterns plus shared practices; a complete library without shared language still produces duplicated inconsistent interfaces
explanation: X.ui|library_vs_system: design_system=patterns+shared_practices(coherently_organized_for_product_purpose); pattern_library=tool_to_capture+share_patterns NOT_the_system_itself; library_alone: corrects_small_inconsistencies BUT_cannot_fix_bad_design+patterns_still_misused; NO:treating_library_completion_as_system_completion; eg: Sipgate_built_thorough_library -> year_later_still_full_of_duplicated_patterns -> missing_shared_practices_not_missing_docs

principle: Add a 3–4px colored left or top border to a plain white card to elevate it without shadows — a single accent line replaces the need for elevation or colored backgrounds
explanation: X.ui|accent_border_card: plain_white_card == bland+indistinct+floating; accent_border: border-left:4px_solid_primary OR border-top:3px_solid; effect: instant_hierarchy+personality without shadow or bg-color; use_for: status_cards+feature_items+highlighted_sections; color_meaning: success=green,warning=amber,info=blue,feature=primary; NO:heavy_shadows_everywhere_for_interest NO:full_card_bg_color_for_all_states; eg: white_card_with_no_identity >> white_card+4px_left_border_in_accent_color

principle: Contrast typefaces across six dimensions — size, weight, structure, form, direction, and color — and combine multiple contrasts for maximum effect rather than relying on a single difference
explanation: X.ui|six_type_contrasts: size: big_vs_small; weight: heavy_vs_light(NOT_medium_vs_medium); structure: monoweight_sans_vs_thick-thin_serif; NO:contrasting_only_medium_weight_with_slightly_heavier; eg: Helvetica_headline+Helvetica_body >> Aachen_Bold_large+Arno_small_serif_body

principle: Separate functional patterns from perceptual patterns in your system — functional patterns support user actions and break usability when changed; perceptual patterns carry brand feel and break coherence; they evolve on different timescales and need different governance
explanation: X.ui|functional_vs_perceptual: functional=user_behavior_support(buttons/forms/nav)->changes_break_usability; perceptual=brand_feel(color/typography/animation/spacing)->changes_break_coherence; perceptual_permeates_whole_system_connecting_modules; brand_refresh->update_perceptual_without_touching_functional; NO:embedding_brand_color_directly_into_functional_component_logic; eg: button_accepts_variant_prop -> perceptual_layer_applies_brand_color_separately

principle: Treat animation timing as a scale with base and increments — two elements animated at the same duration feel completely different if they differ in size or travel distance
explanation: X.ui|animation_timing_scale: timing+easing=two_core_concepts; same_duration=different_perceived_speed_at_different_sizes+distances; solution: base_duration+incremental_steps_like_typographic_scale; small_short-travel=less_than_base; long-travel=base; full-screen=1-2_increments_above; NO:single_fixed_duration_for_all_animations; eg: base_0.5s: icon_scale=0.2s; menu_slide=0.5s; full_screen_transition=0.8s -> consistent_feel_not_consistent_values

principle: Choose typefaces that are either clearly the same (concordant) or clearly different (contrasting) — similar-but-different typefaces create conflict that looks like a mistake rather than a design decision
explanation: X.ui|type_combination_rule: three_states: (1)concordant: one_typeface+its_variations -> calm+formal (2)conflict: two_typefaces_with_similar_characteristics -> looks_like_mistake (3)contrast: two_typefaces_very_different_in_structure+weight+form -> intentional+exciting; diagnosis: wrong_combination -> look_for_SIMILARITIES_not_differences; NO:two_serif_faces_that_look_similar; eg: Opal_Regular+ITC_Baskerville_together >> feels_like_mistake >> Opal+strong_sans_serif >> clear_contrast

principle: Use ease-out for entering elements and ease-in for exiting elements — entering content should decelerate into place, exiting content should accelerate away
explanation: X.ui|easing_direction: ease-out(cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1)): element_starts_fast+decelerates_into_final_position == natural_arrival; ease-in(cubic-bezier(0.4,0,1,1)): element_starts_slow+accelerates_out == natural_departure; linear: mechanical+robotic(never_use_for_UI_motion); NO:linear_for_modal_enter; eg: modal_transition:ease-in >> modal_enter:ease-out+modal_exit:ease-in

principle: Group interface elements by user behavior they support before systemizing them visually — 'Refine a list' uses tabs on some pages and dropdowns on others; behavior-based grouping exposes consolidation candidates that appearance-based grouping hides
explanation: X.ui|behaviors_before_visual: wrong: group_by_visual_type_first; right: identify_behaviors(Discovery+Catalog+Wishlist)->break_into_actions->find_elements_per_action->group_by_purpose; reveals: same_action_solved_by_different_elements=consolidation_candidate; NO:grouping_components_only_by_visual_type_or_page_location; eg: "refine_list"_behavior->tabs_some_pages+dropdown_others -> one_canonical_pattern_identified

principle: Make contrast strong enough to be obviously intentional — a sort-of-heavy line next to a sort-of-heavier line looks like a mistake, not a design decision
explanation: X.ui|contrast_strength: wimpy_contrast: similar_weights+similar_sizes+similar_colors -> looks_like_mistake; effective_contrast: elements_must_be_VERY_different -> no_ambiguity_about_intention; rule: either_same_or_very_different; examples: hairline_vs_4pt_rule NOT hairline_vs_half_point; dark_brown_headline_on_black_body -> no_contrast; NO:dark_brown_on_black_text NO:two_typefaces_that_are_similar; eg: Times_headline+Times_body >> Aachen_Bold_headline+Times_body -> unambiguous_contrast

principle: Effective component names are metaphorical have personality or communicate purpose — the name must create a mental image; if no image forms the pattern will be forgotten and recreated rather than reused from the library
explanation: X.ui|component_naming_qualities: three_qualities: 1_metaphorical(Brackets=supports_content_like_architectural_brackets) 2_personality(Minions=small_secondary_buttons+Boss=primary_CTA) 3_purpose_communicating(Billboard=promotes_anything); test: does_name_create_mental_image? -> no_image=won't_be_remembered=won't_be_reused; NO:technically_precise_names_nobody_visualizes; eg: "Binary_radio_button"(nobody_recalled) >> "Minion"(instantly_associated_with_role+hierarchy)

principle: Settings must be findable by convention (near username, top-right) and organized into named groups — users who hunt for preferences develop resentment toward the product
explanation: X.ui|settings_editor: distinct_from_wizard: non-sequential+user_returns_repeatedly+controls_own_order; findability: link_adjacent_to_username_top-right(pure_convention); organization: named_tabs_or_pages+card-sort_with_users_for_categories; layout: tabs+Two-Panel_Selector+drilldown; NO:settings_buried_in_hamburger_menu NO:one_giant_unsorted_scrolling_list; eg: click_username_top-right -> settings_by_topic -> one_click >> deep_menu_hunt_for_preferences -> user_gives_up

principle: Place a headline closer to the text below it than to the text above it — equal spacing traps white space between head and body and makes them appear unconnected
explanation: X.ui|headline_proximity: equal_space_above_and_below_headline -> head_floats_unconnected -> reader_must_guess_what_it_belongs_to; rule: space_above_headline > space_below_headline; trapped_white_space: white_space_between_head_and_body -> visually_pushes_them_apart; mechanism: closer_proximity_implies_relationship; NO:same_Return_above_and_below_heads NO:auto_spacing_without_checking; eg: rack_card_equal_returns_everywhere >> head_closer_to_its_own_body_copy+more_space_above

principle: Use canvas-plus-palette layout for all creation tools — this idiom is so universal that deviating from it forces relearning even for experts migrating from competing apps
explanation: X.ui|canvas_palette: structure: central_canvas(create+edit)+icon_palettes_at_edges; user_flow: select_palette_tool -> apply_to_canvas; mental_model: digital_workbench; used_by: Photoshop+Figma+Sketch+Keynote+Illustrator; expectations_so_strong: deviation_causes_confusion; NO:mixing_canvas_with_wizard-style_workflow NO:hiding_tools_in_menus_for_graphical_editors; eg: novel_layout_for_image_editor >> standard_canvas+palette -> expert_user_productive_immediately

principle: Naming components by appearance causes library fragmentation — teams cannot find 'Prominent tile' so they create a new one; a year of enthusiastic documentation produces a system still full of duplicates but now they are all thoroughly named
explanation: X.ui|presentational_naming_antipattern: presentation_names(Prominent_tile/Circle_with_dot): unfindable_as_library_grows+no_guidance_where_to_use; Sipgate: thorough_docs+presentation_names -> year_later_too_many_modules -> easier_to_add_new_than_find_existing; NO:naming_by_color(blue_button) NO:naming_by_position(top_left_module) NO:naming_by_size(large_card); eg: "Secondary_promotional_tile"(forgotten) >> "Spotlight"(recalled+reused_across_contexts)

principle: Make the primary completion action the most visually prominent button on the screen — a clear, obvious last step gives users closure and prevents them from abandoning a transaction that's already done
explanation: X.ui|prominent_done: use_when: any_form_submission+transaction_completion+settings_commit+wizard_end; visual_treatment: contrasting_color+generous_surrounding_space+clear_label(Done/Submit/Install/Search); position: near_last_field_or_control(not_far_away); NO:done_button_same_visual_weight_as_secondary_actions; eg: Submit_button_buried_below_secondary_links >> large_contrasting_Submit_button_near_last_field -> user_finds_and_clicks

principle: Carry repetitive visual elements across all brand touchpoints so a business card, letterhead, and envelope are instantly recognizable as belonging to the same person or company
explanation: X.ui|brand_repetition: mismatched_stationery: business_card_looks_unrelated_to_letterhead -> undermines_credibility; solution: identify_2-3_strong_visual_elements(typeface_combination+color+graphic_element) -> apply_consistently_across_all_materials; test: person_who_got_your_card_last_week_should_recognize_your_letter_today; NO:redesigning_each_piece_independently; eg: different_font+color_on_card_vs_letterhead >> shared_bold_typeface+accent_color+logo_across_card+letterhead+envelope

principle: Break the centered alignment default — centered is the beginner's safe choice that creates a formal and often dull look, while flush left or right creates a stronger more sophisticated result
explanation: X.ui|avoid_default_centered: centered_alignment: most_common_beginner_choice -> safe+comfortable+dull; soft_edge: centered_text_has_no_hard_vertical_edge -> weak_invisible_line; flush_left_or_right: hard_edge -> strong_invisible_line_reader_can_actually_see; NO:centering_everything_automatically; eg: report_cover_centered_Times >> flush_left_interesting_typeface+author_separate_but_aligned

principle: Use scroll-triggered animations to reveal content as it enters the viewport — below-fold content that animates in on scroll feels like a progressive reveal, not a page load
explanation: X.ui|scroll_reveal: all_content_visible_immediately -> long_page_feels_dense; scroll_reveal: IntersectionObserver(threshold:0.1) -> add_class_is-visible -> opacity_0_to_1+translateY_20px_to_0; direction: downward_scroll_reveals_from_bottom; NO:parallax_for_decorative_scroll; eg: all_sections_static >> sections_fade-in_20px_up_as_they_enter_viewport

principle: Use strong repetition to earn the right to break the pattern — only a consistently designed publication can use a surprise element that readers will recognize as intentionally special
explanation: X.ui|repetition_then_contrast: surprise_on_inconsistent_page == more_noise; surprise_on_consistent_page == emphasis; mechanism: establish_strong_repetition_first -> reader_learns_the_pattern -> break_it_once_for_critical_item -> reader_notices; NO:breaking_pattern_before_establishing_it; eg: inconsistent_design_with_random_highlights >> consistent_design+single_deliberate_break_for_most_important_element

principle: Brand identity lives in the proportions and relationships between perceptual elements not in individual elements — TED red used sparingly is TED; too much red stops feeling like TED; the ratio matters as much as the color value itself
explanation: X.ui|brand_proportions: brand_feel=relationships+proportions_between_elements NOT_individual_elements; how_colors_relate+how_imagery_relates_to_typography+how_typography_relates_to_spacing; TED: "red_used_sparingly+thoughtfully; wrong_place_or_too_much->doesn't_feel_like_TED"; Spotify_intimacy: right_ratio_green_to_black+subdued_imagery+subtle_interactions together; NO:defining_brand_only_by_hex_color_values; eg: brand_color_applied_everywhere >> proportion_rules_defined -> brand_feel_restored

principle: Pattern descriptions must answer three questions — what it is, what it is for, and how it achieves its purpose; factually correct but purposeless descriptions lead to misuse and duplication
explanation: X.ui|pattern_description: three_questions: 1_what_is_it? 2_what_is_it_for? 3_how_does_it_achieve_purpose?; vague=likely_misuse+duplication; content_rules_per_pattern(max_3_lines_per_fact); NO:factually_correct_but_purposeless_descriptions; eg: Showcase_uses_media_with_multiple_info_types(vague_->_misused) >> Fact_Grid_is_shortlist_of_facts_to_give_reader_immediate_impression_before_content(purposeful->reused)

principle: Stagger list item animations with 30–60ms delays between items — simultaneous list rendering looks like a flash, staggered rendering communicates structure and guides the eye
explanation: X.ui|stagger_animation: all_items_at_once -> flash_not_animation; stagger: each_item_delayed_by_30-60ms_from_previous; duration_per_item: 200-300ms; direction: top_to_bottom(list)+center_out(grid); implementation: CSS:nth-child(n){transition-delay:calc(n*50ms)} OR JS_loop; max_stagger_total: 300ms(beyond_that_last_items_feel_forgotten); NO:stagger_over_1000ms_for_10_items NO:random_delays; eg: all_cards_fade_together >> cards_stagger_50ms_each_top_to_bottom

principle: Signal paragraph breaks with either indent OR extra space, never both — and never indent the first paragraph after a heading since the heading already signals a new section
explanation: X.ui|paragraph_break_signals: indent_means: new_paragraph; space_between_means: new_paragraph; both_together: redundant+messy; NO:indent_AND_extra_space; eg: indented+extra_space_both >> indented_only_OR_spaced_only+no_indent_after_heading

principle: In multi-page documents, use repetition so that any two pages are instantly recognizable as belonging to the same publication — a page 13 with no elements from page 3 breaks cohesion
explanation: X.ui|multipage_repetition: multi_page_NO_repetition: page_13_looks_unrelated_to_page_3 -> publication_loses_cohesive_look; repetitive_elements_across_pages: same_header_font+same_rule_position+same_column_structure+same_color_accent; eg: each_chapter_has_different_header_style >> consistent_header_rule+typeface+color_across_all_chapters

principle: Use accordions when users might need to view multiple content sections at once — unlike tabs they allow any combination to remain open, which makes them ideal for tool palettes users customize to their workflow
explanation: X.ui|accordion: collinear_stack+panels_open_close_independently; vs_tabs: tabs=one_visible >> accordion=any_combination_open; best_for: tool_palettes+two-level_menus+user-customizable_workspaces; varying_heights_per_section: works_well; user_opens_preferred_tools_and_leaves_them_open -> personal_workspace_emerges; NO:accordion_when_only_one_section_should_ever_be_visible(use_tabs) NO:accordion_for_equal-height_uniform_content; eg: Photoshop_Layers+Color+Adjustments_panels -> any_combination_open

principle: Use a wizard for long novel tasks where the designer knows the right order better than the user — always provide Back, show a progress indicator, and never use it for expert daily tasks
explanation: X.ui|wizard: use_when: long+novel+user_unfamiliar+designer_knows_best_order; chunking: group_by_theme+dynamic_branching_based_on_choices; always: Back_button+progress_indicator; failure_mode: wizard_for_frequent_expert_tasks -> frustrating(users_want_control); NO:no_Back_button_in_wizard NO:wizard_for_tasks_user_does_daily; eg: software_install+account_setup+checkout -> good_wizard_candidates >> file_rename_as_wizard -> absurd

principle: Accept multiple input formats and normalize to the correct format silently — never reject a credit card number because the user typed spaces or hyphens between digit groups
explanation: X.ui|forgiving_format: principle: be_liberal_in_what_you_accept -> normalize_to_canonical_format_silently; applies_to: credit_card_numbers+phone_numbers+dates+zip_codes; implementation: strip_spaces+hyphens+dashes -> validate_underlying_digits; NO:rejecting_valid_data_for_formatting_reasons; eg: credit_card_rejects_4444_4444_4444_4444(with_spaces) >> PayPal_accepts_any_separator -> normalizes_automatically

principle: Repeat one visual element throughout the entire design to create unity — repetition is the difference between a collection of items and a coherent design
explanation: X.ui|repetition_unity: scattered_elements == collection_not_design; repetitive_element: bold_font+thick_rule+bullet_shape+color+spatial_relationship+ANY_recognizable_element; function: ties_disparate_parts_into_one_cohesive_piece; NO:each_section_with_different_visual_treatment; eg: three_separate_card_sections_no_connection >> bold_typeface_repeated_across_all_sections_creates_unity

principle: Replace hamburger menus with bottom tab navigation for apps with more than two core destinations — hamburger menus hide navigation and measurably reduce feature discoverability
explanation: X.ux|bottom_tab_navigation: hamburger: hidden_navigation -> users_explore_less(NNG_eyetracking_studies); bottom_tabs: always_visible -> higher_feature_discoverability+faster_switching; tab_count: 3-5_items(fewer->unnecessary; NO:hamburger_for_primary_destinations; eg: hamburger_top_left >> bottom_tab_bar_5_labeled_tabs

principle: Show shipping costs and delivery time on the product page — revealing them only at checkout is the fastest way to lose user trust
explanation:
X.ux|early_shipping_info: hidden_costs_revealed_at_checkout -> cart_abandonment + goodwill_drain; show_on_product_page: shipping_cost + estimated_delivery_date + free_shipping_threshold; generator_rule: product_card_and_product_page_must_include_shipping_line; NO:shipping_info_only_in_cart NO:"calculated_at_checkout" NO:hiding_fees_until_last_step eg: "price_$49_no_shipping_info" >> "price_$49 +

Free_shipping_over_$50_|_Arrives_Thu-Fri

"

principle: Crop all images in a card grid to the same dimensions and aspect ratio to maintain visual rhythm
explanation: X.ux|card_images: varying_image_sizes_in_grid -> broken_rhythm + chaotic_layout; fix: uniform_dimensions + aspect_ratio + object-fit:cover; all_cards_same_image_height; NO:images_with_different_heights_in_same_grid NO:uncropped_images_stretching_cards eg: "product_grid: img_h:120px + img_h:240px + img_h:180px" >> "all_product_images: aspect-ratio:4/3 + object-fit:cover + w:100%"

principle: Show selected count in multi-select dropdown header and always provide a clear-all button
explanation: X.ux|multiselect_dropdown: selected_options -> checkboxes_inside_list; header_shows_count: "Pizza_toppings_(3)" NOT "Pizza_toppings"; clear_all_button_required; keyboard_navigable_for_accessibility; NO:no_count_of_selected NO:no_way_to_clear_all_at_once eg: "Filters" >> "Filters_(3) [x_Clear_all]"

principle: Show a resume-incomplete-task banner on return visits to abandoned flows — users recall unfinished tasks 90% better than completed ones
explanation: X.ux|zeigarnik_resume: abandoned_flow -> user_subconsciously_remembers; on_return: show_resume_banner + last_completed_step + direct_link_to_continue; NOT generic_homepage_CTA NOT start_over_silently; applies_to: checkout + onboarding + multi_step_form + quiz; eg: "homepage_no_mention_of_abandoned_cart" >> "banner: 'Your order is waiting — 2 items, last step: payment'"

principle: Use checkboxes for multiple selection, radio buttons or select for single selection, and always stack options vertically
explanation: X.ux|input_type_selection: multiple_options_selectable -> checkbox; single_option_only -> radio_or_select; stack_options: vertical NOT horizontal -> prevents_miss_clicks + easier_to_read; NO:radio_buttons_for_multi_select NO:horizontal_option_lists eg: "[ ] Red  [ ] Blue  [ ] Green  (horizontal)" >> "[ ] Red\n[ ] Blue\n[ ] Green  (vertical)"

principle: Use multiples of 8 for all spacing and dimensions to ensure consistent scaling across screen densities
explanation: X.ux|8pt_grid: all_spacing + margins + component_dimensions == multiples_of_8 (8/16/24/32/40/48px); fine_details: 4px_increments; aligns_with_screen_pixel_density -> scales_cleanly_across_all_devices; NO:arbitrary_values (11px/13px/17px) NO:mixing_5px_and_8px_in_same_system eg: "padding_top:10px_margin:15px" >> "padding:8px_margin:16px"

principle: Design completion and success screens with the same care as landing pages — order confirmations and thank-you pages are the emotional end that defines how users remember the entire flow
explanation: X.ux|completion_as_peak: completion == emotional_peak + end_of_flow -> defines_memory_of_entire_journey; invest_in: confirmation_pages + success_states + thank_you_screens; include: clear_status + next_steps + personalized_detail + celebration_visual; NO:plain_text_thank_you NO:generic_success_without_next_action eg: "thank_you_for_your_order_period" >> "order_confirmed + delivery_ETA + tracking_link + related_products + confetti_animation"

principle: Direct the model's gaze toward the headline or CTA — when a face looks at the viewer the eye stops there and the copy is missed
explanation: X.ux|pse_gaze_direction: model_looks_at_camera -> user_fixates_on_face -> misses_text+CTA; model_looks_toward_copy -> user_gaze_follows_to_copy; applies_to: hero_banners + landing_page_images + testimonial_photos; NO:hero_image_model_facing_forward_with_key_copy_beside; eg: "model_facing_camera + headline_beside" >> "model_turned_toward_headline + headline_beside"

principle: Use visual emphasis sparingly — too many highlighted elements compete and cancel each other out, and content that resembles ads gets ignored via banner blindness
explanation: X.ux|emphasis_restraint: one_focal_point_per_context -> maximum_impact; too_many_accents: compete -> dilute -> user_sees_noise NOT hierarchy; banner_blindness: content_styled_like_ad -> ignored_even_if_legitimate; change_blindness: user_focused_on_task -> misses_changes_elsewhere; NO:5_colored_badges_on_one_page NO:CTA_that_looks_like_banner_ad NO:every_feature_marked_as_new eg: "homepage_with_3_badges+2_banners+animated_CTA" >> "one_primary_CTA + one_badge_on_key_feature + clean_whitespace"

principle: Display add-to-cart confirmation without leaving the product page — sending users to the cart on every add interrupts browsing and reduces average order value
explanation: X.ux|add_to_cart_ux: redirect_to_cart_on_add: stops_browsing_session -> customer_buys_one_item_and_checks_out; better: mini_cart_drawer_slides_in+"View_cart"_button+"Continue_shopping" -> browsing_continues; cart_icon: badge_updates_with_count; add_feedback: button_morphs_to_checkmark_300ms+returns_to_original; NO:full_page_redirect_on_add_to_cart NO:no_feedback_on_add; eg: redirect_to_/cart >> drawer_mini_cart_with_continue_shopping

principle: Never force registration before purchase — always offer guest checkout as the primary path, registration as optional secondary
explanation: X.ux|guest_checkout: forced_registration_before_purchase == one_of_top_goodwill_killers; user_wants_to_buy_not_create_account; guest_first_rule: primary_CTA == continue_as_guest; registration == secondary_smaller_link; offer_account_creation_AFTER_purchase_on_confirmation_page; NO:"create_account_to_continue" NO:registration_wall_before_checkout NO:hiding_guest_option eg: "or_guest" >> "or_sign_in"

principle: Place headings visually closer to the section they introduce than to the section above — use margin-top twice the size of margin-bottom
explanation: X.ux|heading_proximity: floating_heading_equidistant_between_sections -> brain_cant_parse_which_section_it_belongs_to; rule: heading_margin_top == 2x_heading_margin_bottom; applies_to: all_heading_levels (h1-h6) + card_titles + section_labels; NO:equal_spacing_above_and_below_headings; eg: h2_margin_top_32px + margin_bottom_16px

principle: Never generate welcome text or introductory paragraphs that describe what the user can already see — happy talk wastes space and nobody reads it
explanation: X.ux|kill_happy_talk: happy_talk=="welcome_to_our_amazing_site_where_you_will_find..."; test: cover_text_with_hand->page_loses_nothing==happy_talk->delete; generator_must_never: welcome_paragraphs+section_intros_describing_what_follows+"we_are_pleased_to"_preambles; NO:"welcome_to_our_store"; eg: "Welcome_to_ShopX,_your_premier_destination..." >> [deleted]->page_works_better

principle: Structure every UI color palette with 60% primary, 30% neutral, and 10% accent — use tints for backgrounds and shades for emphasis
explanation: X.ux|60_30_10: primary_60% (tints -> backgrounds + secondary_buttons; shades -> CTAs + emphasis); neutral_30% (text + borders + dividers); accent_10% (highlights + badges + links); tints == primary+white; shades == primary+black; NO:equal_weight_colors NO:more_than_2_accents eg: "5_equal_brand_colors" >> "blue_60%+gray_30%+orange_10%"

principle: Use filled button for primary CTA only, ghost for secondary, text-link for tertiary — align multiple buttons left-to-right by descending importance
explanation: X.ux|button_hierarchy: filled == primary_CTA (one_per_screen); ghost/outlined == secondary; text_link == tertiary; inline_row: left=most_important -> right=cancel; distance_from_reach == lower_prominence; NO:two_filled_buttons_side_by_side NO:cancel_on_the_left eg: "Delete | Cancel" >> "Cancel | Delete (destructive_right)"

principle: Defer to native mobile input methods and never suppress the system keyboard — custom virtual keyboards and blocked autocomplete increase form abandonment on mobile
explanation: X.ux|mobile_input_native: native_keyboard_types: type=email(@ shown); type=tel(numeric_pad); type=number(numpad); type=search(Search_button); inputmode=decimal(decimal_numpad); autocomplete_attributes: enable_autofill(name+email+address+cc); never: suppress_OS_autocomplete; never: block_copy-paste; mobile_form_rule: fewest_fields_possible + native_input_for_each; NO:custom_OTP_keyboard NO:autocomplete=off_on_standard_fields; eg: text_field_for_phone >> <input_type=tel_autocomplete=tel>

principle: Each user arrives at your site with a reservoir of goodwill that depletes with every friction point — hiding phone numbers, punishing input formats and asking unnecessary data drain it faster than any visual design can refill it
explanation: X.ux|goodwill_reservoir: mental_model: each_user_enters_with_reservoir_of_patience; depletes: hiding_support_phone -> user_angrier_when_they_find_it + requiring_dash_format_in_CC_field + asking_data_not_needed_for_task + marketing_bloat_before_content + sloppy_appearance; special_case: single_mistake_can_empty_instantly(registration_wall_before_purchase); NO:hiding_costs_until_last_step; eg: airport_wifi_3_pages_before_price_reveal >> price_visible_on_connection_page

principle: Users imprint on the interface they first learn and resist redesigns even when the new version is objectively better — plan for emotional transition support, not just feature parity
explanation: X.ux|baby_duck_syndrome: imprint: first_learned_UI -> becomes_user_reference_standard; redesign: even_if_measurably_better -> existing_users_report_"worse"_because_habits_broken; negative_feedback != design_failure; NO:killing_old_UI_day_1; eg: "full_redesign_launch+user_outrage" >> "opt-in_new_UI + whats_new_guide + old_UI_available_6_months"

principle: Start mobile layout from 375px with 16px side margins, 8px gutters, and a 4-column grid
explanation: X.ux|mobile_grid: min_frame_375px (iPhone_mini); side_margins_min_16px; gutters_8px; 4_columns; iOS+Android: same_grid; design_smallest_first -> scale_up -> covers_more_devices; NO:designing_mobile_from_414px+ NO:margins_under_16px eg: "mobile_390px_margin_8px" >> "mobile_375px_16px_margins_4col_8px_gutters"

principle: Treat select boxes as the input control of last resort — they hide options behind an extra click, users try to type into them, and confuse focused-state with selected-state
explanation: X.ux|select_last_resort: select_box:hides_options+extra_click+users_try_to_type+confused_focused_vs_selected+cant_pinch_zoom; <5_options->radio_buttons; large_list->autocomplete; space_saving!=justification; NO:select_as_default_for_any_list; eg:destination_picker_select->replaced_with_autocomplete->fewer_errors+faster_entry; GDS_research:"burn_your_select_tags"

principle: Place frequent actions at screen edges — edges act as infinite targets where cursor stops automatically so users cannot overshoot
explanation: X.ux|infinite_targets: screen_edge == wall -> cursor_stops -> zero_overshoot -> target_effectively_infinite_width; place_at_edges: app_dock_bottom + menu_bar_top + scrollbar_right; acquisition_speed: edge_target >> floating_target_same_size; NO:floating_toolbar_away_from_edges NO:offset_menu_from_screen_edge eg: "app_menu_10px_from_top_edge" >> "app_menu_pinned_to_top_edge -> infinite_target"

principle: Make every navigation choice mindless and unambiguous — three effortless clicks equal one click that requires thought
explanation: X.ux|mindless_choice: krug_law_2: click_count_irrelevant BUT cognitive_cost_per_click_matters; mindless_click=user_sees_one_obviously_correct_option; 3_unambiguous_clicks==1_click_requiring_thought; NO:binary_split_where_user_fits_both("Home/Office"); eg: "Products/Solutions/Services"_ambiguous_overlap >> single_clear_category_user_already_uses

principle: Distinguish product functions from features — users install a product for its functions; features reduce friction within a function but cannot substitute for one; no one installs an app solely for fingerprint authentication
explanation: X.ux|function_vs_feature: function=core_capability_user_hires_product_for; feature=implementation_detail_reducing_load_within_function; feature_alone!=install_motivation; when_two_products_have_same_function: lower_energy_cost_wins -> features_decide_the_winner; NO:building_features_without_core_function; eg: top-up_phone_balance=function; select_from_address_book=feature_of_that_function; fingerprint_auth=feature_not_function -> nobody_installs_for_it_alone

principle: Users muddle through interfaces without understanding how they work — every element must be self-evident from appearance alone with no learning required
explanation: X.ux|muddle_through: users_never_read_instructions+never_learn_interface_model+use_things_wrong_but_cope; implication: every_element_communicates_via_appearance NOT documentation; clickable_things: look_clickable(button_shape+underline+pointer_cursor); NO:icon_only_without_label; eg: icon-only_toolbar_30%_wrong_clicks >> icon+text_label_toolbar_correct_on_first_try

principle: Show at least three product images from different angles plus one in-use shot — buyers cannot touch online products and image quantity directly correlates with purchase confidence
explanation: X.ux|product_images: single_front_image: buyer_cannot_assess_product_fully -> uncertainty -> no_purchase; minimum_set: front+side+back+detail_closeup+in_use_context; image_quality: minimum_1000x1000px(enables_zoom); zoom: enable_on_desktop(hover)+pinch_on_mobile; gallery: swipeable_on_mobile+thumbnail_row_on_desktop; video: one_short_demo_video_outperforms_5_static_images; NO:single_product_photo NO:no_zoom_capability; eg: one_front_shot >> 5_angles+zoom+context_photo

principle: Give the 404 page three recovery paths: search, popular pages, and a single primary CTA — a dead-end 404 with only an error message loses the visitor permanently
explanation: X.ux|404_page_design: dead_end_404: visitor_arrived_with_intent -> loses_intent_on_error_page; three_exits: (1)search_bar(most_flexible_recovery) (2)list_of_popular_or_recent_pages (3)primary_CTA_matching_site_goal; tone: human+slightly_playful NOT robotic; technical_message: hide_it(URL_not_found_is_sufficient); NO:404_with_no_navigation NO:technical_server_error_as_user_message; eg: "404_Not_Found" page >> search+popular_pages+"Go_to_homepage"_CTA

principle: Show action results immediately before server confirmation and roll back only on failure — optimistic UI makes apps feel instant even on slow connections
explanation: X.ux|optimistic_ui: user_action->show_result_immediately->process_in_background->rollback_only_on_error; perceived_response: 0ms_instead_of_500-2000ms; NO:waiting_for_server_before_showing_result; eg: click_Like->wait_800ms_server_response >> click_Like->heart_fills_instantly->sync_background->rollback_only_if_offline

principle: Break complex tasks into smaller sequential steps — each step with few choices reduces cognitive load and increases completion rate
explanation: X.ux|task_steps: complex_task_on_one_screen -> overwhelm + abandonment; break_into: sequential_steps_3-5_fields_each -> user_sees_progress + manageable_decisions; wizard_pattern: step1_basics -> step2_details -> step3_confirm; checklist_onboarding: notion_style -> learn_by_doing_one_task_at_a_time; NO:10_field_form_on_one_page NO:all_settings_on_one_screen eg: "checkout_15_fields_one_page" >> "checkout_3_steps: shipping -> payment -> confirm"

principle: Provide captions for all video and transcripts for all audio — never autoplay media with sound, which drowns out screen readers and violates WCAG 1.4.2
explanation: X.ux|media_accessibility: WCAG_1.2.2: captions_required_for_prerecorded_video; WCAG_1.2.1: transcript_for_audio-only; autoplay_with_sound -> WCAG_1.4.2_failure: screen_reader_audio_drowned_out; autoplay_rule: video_OK_only_if_muted; caption_quality: synchronized+accurate+speaker_identified; NO:video_without_captions NO:autoplay_with_audio NO:audio-only_without_transcript; eg: hero_video_autoplay_with_music >> hero_video_autoplay_muted+captions_available

principle: Ask only for the data needed to complete this exact transaction — every extra field yields fewer submissions and more fake data
explanation: X.ux|minimum_data_ask: each_unnecessary_field_signals: (a)clueless_about_web OR (b)harvesting_for_other_purpose; both_damage_trust; three_harms: (1)users_lie(10%_subscribers_named_"Barney_Rubble") (2)fewer_completions(even_slight_length_increase_kills_submit_rate) (3)impression_damage_even_for_compliant_users; NO:postal_address_on_newsletter_signup; eg: 8_field_registration >> 2_fields(email+password)+explain_what_user_gets

principle: Establish visual hierarchy with size and weight before color — readers without color perception must still be able to identify the most important element on the page
explanation: X.ux|visual_hierarchy_priority: hierarchy_tools_in_order: (1)size: biggest=most_important (2)weight: bold=emphasis (3)position: top-left=read_first (4)contrast: high_contrast=draws_eye (5)color: last_layer; color_as_only_hierarchy_signal -> fails_for_color_blind_users(8%_male); test: view_in_grayscale -> hierarchy_must_still_be_clear; NO:two_elements_same_size+weight_competing; eg: same-size_nav+CTA >> CTA_larger+bolder+positioned_prominently

principle: Use a multi-step checkout with a progress indicator — single-page checkouts increase perceived complexity, and users who see how many steps remain are less likely to abandon
explanation: X.ux|checkout_steps: single_page_checkout: all_fields_visible -> overwhelming+intimidating; multi_step: Cart > Shipping > Payment > Confirm -> each_step_small_and_focused; progress_indicator: step_numbers_or_breadcrumb_at_top; back_navigation: allow_editing_previous_steps; guest_checkout: offer_before_account_creation; autofill: enable_on_all_fields; NO:forcing_account_creation_before_payment NO:checkout_with_no_progress_indicator; eg: one_page_all_fields >> 3-step_with_progress+guest_option

principle: Prevent errors before they happen with constraints and confirmations — the best error message is one that never fires
